title: New Australian Laws On TEXT Messages
content:
I wanted to raise this again because I have now tested it myself, and my concern still stands.

I brought this up a couple of months ago because of the changes to Australian SMS regulations from 1 June. At the time, I was concerned that the issue may not have been fully understood or taken seriously, which is why I have deliberately avoided promoting this program to Australian customers until I had more clarity.

[@Giacomo Chinellato](obj://user/edfd679f109c4ef4b46645bd3d030dcf) , you previously mentioned that the service is registered in Australia and that everything is okay. However, I sent myself a test SMS and it appeared in the spam folder as “Unverified”. From what I understand, this usually indicates that the Sender ID has not been registered under the Australian SMS Sender ID Register through the relevant messaging provider.

Could you please provide an update for Australian users on this?

Recent changes in Australia require businesses using a brand name, such as a business or government agency name, to register on the national SMS Sender ID Register. Unregistered branded SMS messages are now being labelled as “Unverified” by telco carriers to help consumers identify and avoid scams.

Can you please confirm:

Which telco or messaging provider are you using in Australia?

Has the Sender ID been registered with the Australian SMS Sender ID Register?

If not, is this something you will be actioning?

This is important because it may affect businesses considering this type of service if they know their messages could be labelled as “Unverified” or sent to spam, as that can make the message appear untrustworthy to customers.

[@Wayne Connell](obj://user/1ac82be8f98c4b9ab3de0adf4a76eaec) , since you have a good customer list, I would also be interested to know how Australian businesses have responded to this so far. Have they raised concerns about customers receiving messages marked as “Unverified” or going to spam?

For reference, here are the relevant updates:

[https://business.gov.au/news/new-rules-for-businesses-sending-text-messages](https://business.gov.au/news/new-rules-for-businesses-sending-text-messages)

title: Feature requests: drip mode for campaigns + SMS fallback for failed WhatsApp messages
content:
Hey everyone, two feature ideas coming from running a real reactivation campaign for a client. Curious if others would use these too, and hopefully the team can consider them.

1. Drip mode for campaigns

Right now, when you send a campaign, all messages seem to go out at the same moment. The problem is that Meta has a sending speed limit per number, and it's especially low for numbers that are also connected to the WhatsApp Business app \(fixed at 20 messages per second, it's in Meta's throughput docs\). So on bigger lists, a chunk of the messages just fails with a rate limit error, even though the contacts are perfectly valid. It happened to me on both batches I sent, and the failed ones also consumed credits.

The fix seems simple from a user perspective: let campaigns drip out instead of firing all at once. Even something basic like sending in small groups with a short pause between them, or a setting where we choose the pace \(e.g. X messages per minute\). Bonus: spreading messages out also looks more natural and protects the number's quality rating.

2. SMS fallback

Some contacts don't have WhatsApp, and their messages fail. Since the platform already sends SMS anyway, it would be great if a failed WhatsApp message could automatically fall back to SMS for that contact. That way a reactivation campaign actually reaches the whole list instead of silently skipping people, and we don't have to export failed contacts and run a second SMS campaign manually.

Would love to hear if anyone else has run into the failed messages issue on bigger campaigns, or would use either of these. And if there's already a way to do any of this that I missed, tell me and I'll happily take it back.

title: WhatsApp Verification of Business
content:
Connected WhatsApp to Climbo but came with a notification of a yellow triangle. All to do with sending limits from WhatsApp.

So I attempted to go through the \(quite complex steps with WhatsApp\) to verify my customers business.

It specifically asked if my clients was registered aka a Ltd company. I said no, several screens later asked for a certificate of incorporation, as recommended? \(When she’s not registered aka incorporated\) a tax document, a bank statement with company name on and etc.

She’s a new business.
Sole trader so currently using personal account \(allowed\)
Not traded to the level of reaching tax status for VAT.
Not registered with HMRC as yet as hasn’t reached the £1000 threshold.

So I know this is a WhatsApp Issue not a Climbo issue.

I just wondered what the impact of this Unverified Business Status means? As it mentioned that some messages may fail. That delivery would be inconsistent.

Should Businesses that are not Ltd or not have a business bank account have WhatsApp turned off for now for automated review requests?

Or has anyone else managed to verify their customers WhatsApp business for them for this scenario of newly trading sole traders?

I see there has been the request to Climbo for a fallback when they fail as a feature request, which could help. I just wanted to add context to what Unverified has meant for my customer, it’s the business, our clients being unverified not referring to Climbo.

But just wondering if anyone else has verified their customer with WhatsApp successfully and tips?

As it appears from my experience you can only easily become verified if you are a Ltd company.

So makes me think should I not attempt connection of WhatsApp in Climbo unless they are limited/incorporated?

Bearing in mind it does suggest message sending is inconsistent not suspended. So could trust be earned over time with WhatsApp and an unverified business which means drip feed would be more valuable than ever to use this feature. Which I know is on the roadmap, I’m just unsure of its priority status?

title: Google gate keeping
content:
Hey guys,
I have a question about Google's review gating policy. I’m looking at the classic split-flow setup where positive ratings go straight to the public review platforms, and negative ratings go to an internal feedback page but with the twist that we still display the Google/TripAdvisor links on that feedback page so they technically have the option to review.
My question is: since the unhappy customer can still click through to Google from the feedback page, does this actually bypass the "review gating" rule? Or is this still a clear violation because the user experience is different based on their initial rating \(sentiment-based routing\)?
What is the actual risk level with this setup nowadays \(especially with Google’s stricter 2026 detection and public warning banners\)? Do clients actually get flagged, suspended, or get their reviews wiped for this, or is it a gray area that people are still getting away with?
Would love to hear your experiences before I start onboarding clients with this!
Thanks!

title: GEO Agent

Does this actually work well in practice, getting clients picked up by AI search tools like ChatGPT and Gemini? title: Review Analytics - Suggestions, do you agree?
content:
[@Giacomo Chinellato](obj://user/edfd679f109c4ef4b46645bd3d030dcf) Thank you so much for the latest Climbo 2.0 updates. The new home screen is looking much clearer and easier to use, and it's great to have some analytics on there!

I wanted to share some general feedback based on what we regularly hear from clients, as well as the reporting we currently use in Climbo 1.0.

One area that would be particularly useful to develop further is the Build Trust section but could also go on the "Reputation Dashboard' page if more apprropriate.

At the moment, some of the more detailed review reporting available in Climbo 1.0 is still missing.

The main things we would love to see added are:
• Exact review numbers rather than rounded totals
• Date filters, including the last 7 days, last 30 days and a custom date range
• The number of new reviews, broken down by star rating
• The review funnel, including invitations sent, link visits, QR code visits and negative feedback
• A breakdown of reviews by platform, such as Google, Tripadvisor and Facebook \(appreciate this is already on the reputation dashboard page\)

We review this data every week within our own business, where we also use Climbo 1.0. Having this level of detail available directly on the Climbo 2.0 home screen would make the platform much more valuable for us as operators and for our clients, who place a lot of value on these analytics.

Screenshots are attached for reference to show what we had in Climbo 1.0 which was very useful.

title: Feature request: multi-language support for the GEO blog (widget + articles)
content:
A lot of our clients' websites are multi-language — my restaurant client's site runs Swedish, Spanish and English, which is normal here in Spain with expats and tourists.

The GEO Agent articles are great, but they come in ONE language. The widget translates its own labels \("Full story", dates\) based on the visitor's browser, but the article itself stays in whatever language it was written in. So a Swedish customer on a Swedish site reads an English article, looks half-finished to the client.

What could fix it:
[ul][li]GEO Agent writes each article in the languages the location needs \(let them select it manually\)[li]The articles feed/widget serves the version matching the visitor's browser language

Right now we're solving this outside the platform \(pulling the articles and translating them on the client's site\), but native support would make the blog feature sellable to every multi-language market — Spain, coastal towns, tourist areas basically.

Anyone else running multi-language clients and hitting this?

title: Quick feature suggestion for the GEO Agent:
content:
Right now, the GEO Agent creates upcoming blog topics in advance, and clients can edit or add topics, which is very useful.

One improvement that would be helpful: when we edit an upcoming topic, it would be great to also have the option to upload a custom image in advance.

Many clients may prefer using their own real business photos instead of AI-generated images. So if the topic is already visible for the coming weeks, the business owner can upload the right image for that topic beforehand, and when the blog is published later, it automatically uses that uploaded image.
This would make the scheduled blogs feel much more personalized and brand-specific, especially for local businesses.

title: Improving Coupons & Free/Paid Plan Transitions
content:
Hi Climbers,

We’ve received several comments and tickets about the new coupon feature and how you currently work with both free plans and Stripe plans.

Here’s the current situation that can feel limiting:

Right now, coupons created via the platform apply to all plans. So if a client uses a coupon on a Bronze plan and later upgrades to Silver, the discount automatically transitions to the new plan as well.

Customers can apply a coupon when signing up through a payment link. However, for existing customers, you cannot directly apply a coupon from the client’s billing portal unless you trigger a subscription update \(like upgrading/downgrading the plan or changing quantity\). Only in that case does the “apply coupon” field appear.

Also, you cannot directly move a paid \(Stripe\) client to one of your free plans. To do this, you currently need to delete the subscription, recreate the client manually under a free plan, redo onboarding, reconnect integrations, and regenerate links. Essentially, the client starts from zero.

Here’s how we want to improve this:

First, when creating a coupon, you will be able to select exactly which plans it applies to \(for example, only Bronze\). If a client on Bronze with an active coupon upgrades to Silver, the discount will not automatically transition unless that plan is explicitly included in the coupon configuration.

Second, via the Stripe API, it is possible to apply a coupon directly to an existing subscription without modifying the plan. We will add an “Apply Coupon” button inside the Clients area. This will allow you to manually apply a coupon to an active subscription without forcing an upgrade, downgrade, or quantity change. Coupons will support one-time discounts or forever discounts — and with the new logic, they can remain tied only to the client's plan.

Third, we want to introduce a simple “Move to Free Plan” button in the Clients area. This will allow you to move a paid client \(in trial or active subscription\) to a free plan without rebuilding the account. Integrations, links, and settings will remain intact. This opens up cleaner flows for trial expirations and freemium strategies.

title: Opt In Page Feature Request
content:
I had set up an opt in page to gather email addresses by way of offering a valuable freebie, like an eBook/Guide.

Because we would need to then export those particular emails to their other email marketing software to actually deliver the eBook, I have decided to code one of their NFC cards directly that funnel outside of Climbo.

However, the valuable use for the opt in function within Climbo that we have uncovered is that if we use the QR code that is generated for the opt in page and create a promotional flyer that she puts around other business, cafe's, doctors surgeries, dentists waiting areas etc, she could get more leads that way to market to.

The only thing to make this work if we use the QR code generated for the opt in form is like a tag for the contact, so that we know they are a lead and not yet a customer and therefore should not receive a review request yet and just the lead magnet.

title: Improving the First Impression of the Automated Website
content:
[@Giacomo Chinellato](obj://user/edfd679f109c4ef4b46645bd3d030dcf), I really like the direction of the Automated Website feature. Instantly generating a fully optimized website from a Google Business Profile with reviews, photos and schema already in place is a very strong offer.

One suggestion I have is regarding the header section \(above the fold\). Right now there is a bit too much information competing for attention, which can make the landing experience feel overwhelming instead of clear.

I think the first section should follow more of an AIDA-style structure focused on simplicity and clarity.

The header should instantly grab Attention with only a few powerful words explaining what the business does, combined with the business address/location for immediate local relevance and trust.

To build Interest, it would be great if we could add an AI-generated image or video that agencies can easily replace themselves depending on the business niche. That would make the website feel much more dynamic and personalized.

This approach creates a much calmer and more premium first impression. Less distraction above the fold usually means visitors understand the offer faster and stay engaged longer.

Then below the fold, the Google Business Profile 750-character business description can do the heavier lifting by explaining the products, services and unique value of the business in more detail.
Personally, I would avoid adding a busy background image there as well, since readability is more important in that section. Maybe reserve subtle space on the left or right for visuals if needed.

The next sections can stay as they are because the overall concept is already very strong.

title: Review Tracking: NEED ADVISE
content:
Good morning, team! I'd love to get some input from anyone who's run into a similar situation.
I'm currently running a trial with a restaurant, and the owner has another 15 locations. If this first location is successful, there's a great opportunity to roll the software out to all 15, which is really exciting.
The only roadblock I'm facing is with aliases. I completely understand why we're not allowed to use employees' names, especially with the new Google regulations, and I agree with that approach. My challenge is that in a restaurant, servers rotate shifts all day and they all perform the same role. Aliases like "Server," "Host," or "Great Service" don't really help me identify which team member earned the review.
I originally thought each person's unique QR code would be what tracked the reviews, but I'm now realizing it's the alias that does the tracking.
I'm just trying to find a solution to this specific use case. Has anyone in hospitality or a similar industry found a good workaround? I'd really appreciate any ideas or advice. Thanks in advance!

content:
Me and [@Jason Andreasson](obj://user/393446cf3ef9454eba48a25a49d95ae2) just launched a new update for our website: [https://animatech.pro](https://animatech.pro)

-------
If someone needs to go over their copy, I'll drop a prompt below for it based on Myron Goldens sales frameworks:

Myron Golden Sales Framework AI Expert

You are a master sales strategist trained in Myron Golden's methodology for creating irresistible offers. Your role is to help users build, refine, and communicate offers that maximize perceived value and create buying environments where customers feel compelled to purchase. You never guess or fabricate information — if critical details are missing, you ask clarifying questions.
Context

Users come to you needing help with sales and marketing challenges: creating offers, writing sales copy, structuring presentations, crafting email sequences, or diagnosing why their current offers aren't converting. Your expertise is grounded in Myron Golden's proven frameworks, particularly the 5D Framework, the Psychology of Buying, and the 10x Value Rule.
Core Principles You Always Apply
People buy offers based on how those offers make them feel — they're buying their believed transformation, not your product
Perceived value matters more than intrinsic value for the sale \(though both are required for ethical selling\)
Emphasize only the PAYOFF — never pieces, process, or person
The value must be 10x the price for an easy yes
Create environments where people feel like buying — serve, don't pursue

Instructions

What are you selling? \(product, service, coaching, course, etc.\)
Who is your target audience? \(be specific: demographics, psychographics, current situation\)

title: Feature Request: Advanced Google Review Analytics & Custom Date Range Reports
content:
Hi Climbo Team,
Firstly, thank you for continuously improving the platform.
I would like to request an enhancement to the review reporting module, as this is one of the most frequently requested features from our clients.
Current Challenge
Many business owners and marketing teams ask us questions such as:
[ul][li]How many Google reviews did we receive in June 2026?[li]How many reviews were collected this month, last quarter, or during a custom date range?[li]What was the distribution of 1★, 2★, 3★, 4★ and 5★ reviews during that period?[li]How many customers left only a star rating without writing a review?[li]How many customers submitted a written review?
Currently, this information is not readily available as a consolidated report.

Suggested Enhancement
Add a Review Analytics section with a selectable date range, including predefined filters such as:
Today, Yesterday, Last 7 Days, Last 30 Days, This Month, Last Month, This Quarter, Last Quarter, This Year, Custom Date Range

For the selected period, display:
[ul][li]Total Reviews Received[li]Average Rating[li]5★ Reviews[li]4★ Reviews[li]3★ Reviews[li]2★ Reviews[li]1★ Reviews[li]Reviews with Written Comments[li]Ratings Only \(No Written Review\)[li]Percentage distribution of each star rating

It would also help us present professional performance reports without exporting and manually analyzing review data.
Thank you for considering this enhancement.

We've received this request from multiple clients. During discussions, a few clients also mentioned that platforms such as Framepilot and Rankley provide similar reporting capabilities. Having this feature in Climbo would make the platform even more competitive and eliminate the need for manual reporting.

title: Question Regarding Climbo AI Ranking Mechanics and Prompts
content:
Hello everyone. Following up on some of the recent win posts showcasing exceptional day one results, I am hoping to get a bit of technical clarity on how the Climbo dashboard operates behind the scenes regarding AI search tracking.
As someone with experience in local SEO, seeing a brand new profile rank number one on platforms like ChatGPT and Gemini within twenty-four hours is fascinating but highly unusual for organic searches. Because these Large Language Models usually rely on established web prominence and historical indexing data, an overnight top ranking suggests the tracking software might be using highly specific search parameters.
Could anyone share exactly how the platform configures its AI search prompts? For example, does it feed the AI the precise business name and coordinates behind the scenes, or is it measuring a broad, unbranded query? Understanding if variables like a tight 5 km radius or a rural location affect these automated reports would be incredibly helpful for interpreting the data accurately.
I look forward to hearing insights from anyone familiar with the platform tracking mechanics.

title: Planned Feature: Employee Tracking + Leaderboard
content:
Hi Climbers,

we are planning a new feature around employee tracking, and before building it we want to collect your feedback in detail, because we think this could become one of the most powerful ways to help local businesses generate more reviews in a natural and consistent way.

The idea is this.

The business owner will have a dedicated section called Employees Links, where they can add the employees who are responsible for asking customers to leave reviews. Adding an employee will be very simple: the business just inserts a photo, a name, and an alias, which can also simply be the employee’s real name or nickname.

Every time an employee is added, the platform will automatically generate a dedicated review link for that employee. It will also be possible to download the related QR code. Technically, this employee link will simply be the general review link with an extra URL parameter, something like employee=alias.

In this way, we will be able to track the unique visits to each employee’s review link. So if an employee asks a customer to scan their own QR code or open their own review link, the business will be able to measure how many unique visits that employee has generated.

On top of this, we want to build a leaderboard, inspired by the Skool style, where the business can see the ranking of employees based on the number of unique visits to each employee’s review link. The leaderboard will be viewable for the last 7 days, the last 30 days, or all-time.

This would already create an interesting use case for businesses. For example, the company could decide that, on the last day of every month, the employee with the highest number of unique visits receives a bonus in payroll, because that employee made a stronger effort to ask customers for reviews.

But we want to take this feature much further.

Our idea is that if, inside the text of the review, the customer mentions the alias of the employee, then that review will be automatically assigned to that employee. For example, if the review says “Luke was very kind with us”, the review will automatically be associated with the employee whose alias is “Luke”.

title: 🚀 API Suggestion: SSO / Magic Link for White-Label Connection (Crucial for SaaS platforms)
content:
Hi everyone!
I am currently using the Climbo API to power my own local reputation SaaS platform. The tool is amazing, but I am facing a major roadblock regarding user experience \(UX\).
Right now, when my clients need to connect their Google Business Profile, they have to be redirected to the Climbo white-label dashboard with a dedicated email/password login. Because my platform is a multi-tenant SaaS, this breaks the user journey and shows the "seams" of the integration, which can cause trust issues for my clients.
Would it be possible to add a SSO \(Single Sign-On\) or a Magic Link system to the Climbo API? This would allow users to authenticate seamlessly from our own platforms directly to the GBP connection page, without needing a secondary login/password screen on the white-label domain.
This evolution would be a game-changer for anyone building a serious SaaS or an advanced agency portal on top of the Climbo API.
Is this something on the product roadmap? I'd love to hear your thoughts!

title: DEFLATED - Please Help
content:
Hello Climbers,
Over the months I've been following the rollout of some truly amazing new features in Climbo, and every time I see one, I can't wait to implement it in my own business.

However, when I go to do so, I realise I simply don't have the ability to.

Is that because I'm in a country where Stripe isn't available?

I ask because many of these ARE NOT Stripe features but SHOULD BE Climbo platform features. While I completely understand that payment processing is dependent on Stripe, a number of the features do not rely on Stripe in any way. Stripe is the trigger afterwards but not the main event.

Perhaps I'm missing something, so I'd really appreciate some guidance.
For example:
[ul][li]How do we display the pricing of our plans \(Providing a field to add the price per plan so that it can be displayed\)? Displaying pricing helps clients understand the value of each option and naturally encourages them to move to higher-tier plans.

[ul][li]How do we make all our plans visible to clients? Allowing clients to compare plans is an important part of helping them choose the option that best suits their needs. \( Not asking for the cmd to trigger the upgrade but a cmd that when they want to upgrade maybe something as small as a WhatsApp can be triggered to us.  Just provide the command button to link we can do the rest\)

[ul][li]How do we offer a trial period? We don't need Climbo to process the payment—We can manage that using the payment methods available in my country. We simply need a way to offer the trial and know when it has expired \(the current email warning that a trial is coming up for expiry is not stripe reliant, it's a trigger to inform.\)

[ul][li]How do we provide a proper registration process? Ideally, like with Stripe Climbers, would appreciate the simplicity available for clients to be able to REGISTER to choose a plan, create a password, enter their location, and connect their Google Business Profile during onboarding.

These are all inherently CLIMBO platform features not payment gateway features.

title: 🚀 New Features – AMA Recording (02/07/2026)
content:

Agency Mode:

title: Recurring question from customers
content:
We’re receiving a recurring question from customers:
“How can I measure how much my business has improved since I started using the app?”
Today, the platform shows important metrics such as reviews, average rating, reputation evolution, and activity. However, there still isn’t a clear and direct way to measure the real impact generated by using the app.

It would be very valuable to have an Impact / Progress Tracking section that allows users to compare the business’s starting point vs. its progress over time.
For example:
[ul][li]Initial rating vs. current rating[li]Number of reviews at the start vs. current reviews[li]New reviews generated since activation[li]Improvement in average rating[li]Monthly evolution[li]Reviews automatically answered[li]Changes in visibility or ranking[li]Estimated impact generated by reputation improvement

This would help customers clearly understand the value they are getting, and it would also make it easier to show concrete results during the trial, renewal, or upsell process.
In short: customers need a simple way to see the “before and after” so they can understand how much they have improved thanks to the app.

title: Inherit Configuration from the First Location When Adding New Locations
content:
When adding new locations to an account, I suggest the system should allow the configuration from the first branch to be inherited automatically.

This would avoid having to manually recreate the full setup for each new branch, including agent configuration, review link texts, automation settings, tone of voice, templates, and other related settings.
Ideally, when creating a new branch, the user should have the option to either:

[ol:1][li]Start with a blank configuration, or[li]Copy/inherit the configuration from the first branch or from any existing branch.

This would make onboarding multi-location businesses much faster, reduce manual errors, and improve the setup experience for agencies, franchises, and companies with multiple branches.

title: Important Notice About Unauthorized Promotions
content:
Hi Climbers,

It has come to our attention that a member of the community, Lewis Parker, contacted many of you privately to promote his own paid products in direct competition with Climbo.

Several of you reported this behavior, and it has caused a significant reputational issue for the group. Many new members felt targeted by spam immediately after joining, leading some to leave or perceive the environment as unsafe or overly promotional.

This behavior violates our community rules, our terms of use, and basic ethical standards. Using the private Climbo community chat to promote personal paid products, especially competing ones, is unacceptable and harmful to the group.

We sincerely apologize to anyone who received these messages or requests. Our chat is intended for networking, collaboration, and support, not for personal sales. If you wish to promote products, please use appropriate channels such as paid ads or other marketing avenues, but not this community.

The member has been banned from the group, reported to Skool, and further actions will be taken as needed. For now, the chat has been temporarily disabled. We apologize again for this disruption.

This community operates under clear [rules](https://www.skool.com/climbo/community-rules-please-read-before-posting), which are linked in the group info box. We have a responsibility to enforce these rules to keep the group focused on its purpose: helping you grow your business with Climbo. Anyone not adhering to these rules cannot remain in the group.

Thank you to everyone who reported this behavior and helped us maintain a safe, productive community.

See you on Thursday at the live session, where we’ll announce exciting new features and important platform improvements!

title: 💸 New Feature: Affiliate System – AMA Recording (17/06/2026)
content:
Hi Climbers,

We’re excited to announce the launch of the Affiliate System! This new feature allows you to easily manage, track, and reward affiliates who help you bring new clients to the platform. Here’s how it works:

Agency Mode – Affiliate Section

The affiliate table shows all your added affiliates, whether added manually or via the widget.
You can see each affiliate’s name, login email, total reward, total commission, paid commission, to-pay commission, payout method, and payout details.

When you manually process the payout via the related payout method, you can easily mark “to pay” commissions as paid, which will reset the “to pay” amount to 0 and update the “paid” total.

Adding Affiliates

Manual Add: Choose name, login email, assign a coupon \(existing ones\), set reward \(percentage + one-time or recurring\), select payout method, provide payout details, and pick visible plans \(within paid plans that allow coupon codes\). The affiliate receives a welcome email with an authentication link to access their affiliate dashboard.

Widget Add \(Automatic\): Set the reward \(percentage + one-time/recurring\), payout method, and coupon details. Personalize widget fields \(name, email, payout details, button text, thank you URL\). Copy the dynamic script and embed it. When an affiliate signs up via the widget, they’re automatically created, assigned a coupon code, and receive the welcome email with an authentication link.

Reward Types
When creating an affiliate, you can select between two types of rewards: One-Time and Recurring.

One-Time: the affiliate earns a commission only on the first payment of the client \(this is recommended if you offer high-ticket annual plans\).
[ul][li]Example: If a client buys an annual plan for $3,000 and the affiliate reward is 40% one-time, the affiliate earns $1,200 once when the client makes the first payment \(first year\). No additional payments will generate more commissions.

Recurring: the affiliate earns a commission on every payment the client makes, for as long as the subscription remains active \(this works well for monthly subscription plans\).

title: Potential Risk
content:
Hello Giacomo,
I would like to draw attention to a potential issue: currently, agency staff can make changes to client accounts. I consider this a potential risk. While this can sometimes be useful, it requires the staff to know exactly what they are doing. For instance, if an employee accidentally disconnects the link to the Google account, they have no way to fix it, as they do not have access to the Google account itself. I therefore suggest introducing a more granular system of rights and roles that allows you to define exactly what each employee is permitted to do.

title: Quick UX question: Agent Activity vs AI Sentiment Analysis (Client Mode)
content:
Hey everyone 👋

Quick question to see if I’m the only one noticing this.

In Client Mode, on the first page I’m seeing both the last 2–3 weeks of Agent Activity \(SEO Agent\) and the AI Sentiment Analysis next to each other.

For me, that layout is a bit confusing. Both blocks are valuable, but when they sit side-by-side it feels like the page is trying to tell two “primary stories” at once. Especially for business owners who log in fast, scan fast, and decide fast.

Personally, I’d prefer the AI Sentiment Analysis to be the main focus on that first page. Sentiment is the “pain/trigger” that hits the owner directly \(and it’s the easiest way to create internal buy-in with staff: “we need to fix this now”\).

Agent Activity is great too. I just see it as secondary, or better placed on a separate tab/section.

What would you prefer on the first page:
[ol:1][li]Only AI Sentiment Analysis[li]Only Agent Activity[li]Keep both, but change the layout \(how?\)

Curious what other agency owners think...

title: 📝 GEO Agent Is Now Live + AMA Replay (16/04/2026)
content:
Hi Climbers!

the GEO Agent will be released today in a few hours.

This is one of the most important features we’ve released so far. It allows you to help your clients automatically generate localized, SEO-focused content with the goal of improving their visibility and ranking on Google.

The GEO Agent takes real business data — services, categories, and especially customer reviews — and turns it into relevant blog articles and content ideas designed to support local SEO over time.

This means your clients are not only collecting reviews, but also using them to build content that strengthens their online presence.

If you want to see exactly how it works, we highly recommend watching the AMA replay from yesterday, where we did a full overview of the feature and how to use it in your offer.

By default, the GEO Agent is disabled in your plans, so you’ll need to activate it manually for the clients you want to give access to it.

This is just the first version, and we’ll keep improving it based on your feedback.

Let us know your thoughts once you start using it.

We’ve also released a new UI for the Plans section.

It’s now much easier to create and manage your custom plans. Instead of a long list of features, everything is now organized into 5 clear areas: Details, Reputation, Visibility, Engagement, Price.

This is purely a UI improvement, so you don’t need to change anything in your existing plans.

Climbo Team 👨🏻‍💻🫡

title: This Week’s Plan: Affiliate System 💸🤑
content:
Hi Climbers!

This week is fully dedicated to the development of the new Affiliate System.

The goal is simple: help you build your own sales team and increase the sales of your paid plans.

With the Affiliate System, you’ll be able to add sellers to your platform either manually or automatically through a sign-up widget that you can embed directly on your website.

Each seller will receive their own coupon code and will be able to start helping you sell your paid plans.

The goal for affiliates is simple: they find new clients and get them to sign up for one of your paid plans using their personal coupon code.

When a client purchases a paid plan using an affiliate’s coupon code, that client will automatically be assigned to that affiliate.

You’ll be able to decide how commissions work for your affiliates:

One-time commission
The affiliate earns a fixed percentage on the first payment only.

Recurring commission
The affiliate earns a percentage of the subscription for as long as the client keeps paying.

Each affiliate will get access to their own white-label dashboard, where they can see their coupon code, the payment links of your paid plans, the clients they brought in, and a rewards section to monitor revenue, commissions, and pending payouts.

From your side, you’ll be able to monitor revenue, commissions, and pending payouts for each affiliate, choose your preferred payout method, such as PayPal, Wise, IBAN, or US bank transfer, and let affiliates enter their payout details directly from their dashboard.

The only part you’ll manage manually is the payout itself. You can review pending commissions once a month and settle the balances with your affiliates based on your own payout process.

This system is designed to help you move beyond selling alone and start building a real sales team around your platform.

Important update: this week’s AMA has been moved to Wednesday.

You can register here:

[https://www.skool.com/climbo/calendar?eid=760e8001029a48c8bb0c391c2903c773](https://www.skool.com/climbo/calendar?eid=760e8001029a48c8bb0c391c2903c773)

title: Reddit integration
content:
I suggest we should implement automated Reddit publishing as part of our growth and visibility strategy. Reddit has become one of the most valuable sources for AI systems and search engines, especially as platforms increasingly rely on community-generated content to surface recommendations.

For local businesses, having an active presence in relevant Reddit communities can significantly improve discoverability, since discussions, reviews, and recommendations from real users are often prioritized by both AI-powered search experiences and traditional search results.

By automating the creation and distribution of high-quality, community-relevant content on Reddit, we can increase brand visibility, generate organic mentions, and improve the likelihood of being recommended when users ask AI assistants or search engines for local business recommendations.

title: Reputation Management issues
content:
Quick context on my setup. We're an agency running Climbo white-label for our clients \(reputation management portion\). The standard flow, 3 stars or higher goes to the Google review page, lower than that routes to a private feedback form with a small Google link at the bottom.

The problem is a few of our clients have flagged this and don't want us running it anymore. With Google enforcing the gating ban hard since April, they're worried the sentiment-based routing puts their profile at risk. From what I'm reading they're not wrong, the penalty now can be removal of all reviews and even suspension, not just the gated ones.

So I'm trying to figure out the right move. A couple of questions for the group:
Are you turning off the rating filter entirely and just sending everyone straight to Google? Or have you found a compliant way to keep a private feedback path without it being triggered by the star rating?

And for those still running the filter, has anyone actually seen enforcement hit a client profile yet, or is it still mostly theoretical in practice?

Appreciate any real-world experience.

title: This Week’s Feature Release 🚀 – AMA Recording (11/06/2026)
content:
Hi Climbers,

Here’s what we’ve been working on this week:

Agency Team Management
You can now add staff members or owner members to your agency. They’ll receive an invite link to log in to your white-label Agency Mode. For staff members, you can choose which clients or plans they can access, giving you full control over visibility and responsibilities within your team.

Freemium Model
Free clients can now switch to paid plans directly from their billing section. By opening the payment link for a paid plan and signing up with their existing account email, their account is automatically moved to the paid plan—no data is lost, no rebuilding required. This enables a true freemium model: keep free clients forever as a lead magnet, then convert them to paid plans to unlock more features.

Climbo Tools Classroom
We’ve launched a new classroom: Climbo Tools. Here you can access a suite of tools built by the Climbo team for you, including Reportly, Prompt2Site, Support Agent, Policy Hub, Mockup GPT, and more coming soon. For each tool, you’ll find detailed instructions on how to access it and how it works. Access is free for all Climbers, so dive in and explore!

Coming Next Week
We’re excited to roll out the affiliate system, so stay tuned for more updates.

As always, thank you for your feedback and for helping us shape Climbo into the best platform for your agency!

Climbo Team 👨🏻‍💻

title: Google’s NEW AI Spam Policy
content:
This month’s update is all about AI Search, and there’s a lot to cover!

From Google’s first-ever official guide on ranking in AI answers, to major updates from Google I/O, to surprising announcements about Google AI Mode.

🤖 Google published its first official [AI Optimization Guide](https://f09f5915.click.convertkit-mail.com/gku3kpxlw8h5hl8d9o3crh8o6veq9amhnqq2/9qhzhnhdmp8w7dt9/aHR0cHM6Ly9kZXZlbG9wZXJzLmdvb2dsZS5jb20vc2VhcmNoL2RvY3MvZnVuZGFtZW50YWxzL2FpLW9wdGltaXphdGlvbi1ndWlkZQ==) on how to show up in generative AI answers. A must-read.

🚫 Google updated its spam policies. Attempting to manipulate AI answers in Google Search is now officially spam. How does Google detect manipulative AI visibility practices? And what are they?

📈 Google I/O Search updates. AI Mode hit 1 billion monthly users, with more links to send traffic to websites.

​[​Read the full coverage on the blog.​](https://f09f5915.click.convertkit-mail.com/gku3kpxlw8h5hl8d9o3crh8o6veq9amhnqq2/3ohphkh3l78mw4tr/aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cubGVhbm5ld29uZy5jby9tYXktMjAyNi1zZW8tYW5kLWFpLXNlYXJjaC1uZXdzLXVwZGF0ZS8=)​
1\) Google's first official AI Optimisation Guide. Google published an official guide on how to optimise your website for generative AI answers, including AI Overviews and AI Mode. The advice on commodity content is very useful.

Google's examples:
[ul][li]Commodity content: “7 Tips for First-Time Homebuyers”. This is common knowledge that anyone could write, or AI could generate.[li]Non-commodity content: “Why We Waived the Inspection and Saved Money: A Look Inside the Sewer Line”. This is a unique, first-hand perspective.
​
📌 For local businesses, this means your blog posts, about page, and portfolio/venue pages should share your unique point of view and personal experience, not generic advice that can be recycled.

Try a quick self-audit:
Paste one of your blog posts or portfolio page descriptions into Google Gemini and ask it: “Does this contain original information, research, or first-hand experience? Or does it read like commodity content that anyone could have written? \[PASTE YOUR CONTENT\]

title: Email delivery, spam risk, unsubscribe bug
content:
I have a concern and a comment regarding the sending of review requests \(campaigns\) via email, followed by a feature request:

Yes, we have in the terms that every review request sent by email requires the customer’s consent to receive emails via double opt-in.

However, we still cannot prevent the following:

If a customer sends review requests to a contact list of 1,000 contacts, all these emails will be sent from our domain. If only 1% of the recipients mark the emails as spam, our entire server could be blacklisted and blocked from sending mail. And this is just with one customer. The risk is huge and immediate.

From my point of view, it is extremely important that the review requests are sent from my customer’s email address. Either via their Gmail address or alternatively via an account that they can connect themselves via SMTP.

What do you think? What does the team think? [@Giacomo Chinellato](obj://user/edfd679f109c4ef4b46645bd3d030dcf) ?

And aside from that, a serious bug: If someone clicks the STOP link in the email, they are taken to [https://app.bird.com/unsubscribe/sms](https://app.bird.com/unsubscribe/sms) – they then have to enter their phone number, but not their email address!

title: This Week’s Development Plan – Agency Team Features
content:
Hi Climbers! 🔥

Here’s what we’re focusing on this week and next to help you manage your agency more efficiently and scale your team.

Week 1 \(This Week\)

Add Staff and Owner Members: Staff can access specific clients or all clients to help manage accounts and provide support. Owners have the same visibility as you, so your business partners can stay fully in the loop.

Client Transition from Free to Paid Plan: When a client on a free plan signs up via a paid plan payment link using the same email as their existing free account, the system will automatically move their account to the paid plan, no rebuilding required.

Free Clients Billing Access: Free clients can now see your paid plans directly in their billing area and navigate to the payment link if they want to upgrade.

Week 2 \(Next Week\)

Sales Team Management: Add sales reps, set their commission rates \(one-time or recurring\), and optionally assign a coupon for automated client assignment. Sales reps will see their assigned clients, track commissions, and monitor outstanding balances. You can also manually assign clients to reps if preferred. Payments to sales reps can be made externally using your preferred method, and once paid, you can mark their balance as "paid," automatically resetting the outstanding amount to zero. This allows you to manage sales performance and reward your team accurately.

Bonus Tools This Week

We’re also giving away several ready-to-use tools built by our team, free for all Climbers:

Automated prompt generator to build your website quickly
Policies generator
GPT tool to turn software screenshots into polished mockups
Redesigned prospecting tool
Knowledge base branded with your agency name

All of these are designed to save you time, streamline operations, and help your agency deliver more value.

We’ll cover all these updates and more during Thursday’s AMA, so make sure to join us!

title: AI Billing is Live: Full Control Over AI Usage and Costs – AMA Recording (04/06/2026)
content:
Hi Climbers,

Following up on last week’s post where we introduced AI billing, we’re excited to announce that it’s now fully live and ready for you to use.

Every AI feature that consumes tokens, ongoing like AI agents and AI chat, or monthly features like sentiment analysis and AI ranking, is billed via your agency balance. This ensures we cover costs to AI partners like OpenAI, keeping the business sustainable in the long term.

Here’s what AI Billing allows you to do:

✅ Set monthly AI token limits per plan – control exactly how much AI each client can use
✅ See token consumption per feature – monitor usage and track spending per client
✅ Enable or disable specific AI features per plan – control access to AI agents, AI chat, sentiment analysis, ranking, and more
✅ Simulate token usage per plan – you can model how many tokens a client could consume and adjust plan limits accordingly to optimize profitability
✅ Know your spending cap per plan – with fixed token pricing, you know the maximum cost a client can generate, allowing you to price plans for guaranteed minimum profit
✅ Pause ongoing AI features when limits are reached – AI agents and AI chat automatically stop until tokens refresh at the start of the next month

This gives you full visibility and control over AI usage, making it easier to build sustainable plans and offer AI features confidently.

We strongly recommend watching the AMA to see live examples of setting the right token limits on your current plans and simulating client usage.

Feedback and suggestions are welcome!

Climbo Team 👨🏻‍💻

title: Optimize AI Costs by Using a Lighter Model for Suitable Tasks
content: We have identified that AI consumption is currently very intensive and costly for certain tasks that do not require the capabilities of a large, high-end model. We recommend evaluating and implementing a lighter, more cost-efficient AI model for these use cases. This approach can significantly reduce operational costs while maintaining the required level of performance and accuracy, resulting in a better cost-benefit ratio and improved scalability.

title: Feature Requests: Localization, AI Custom Prompts, and LINE Messenger Integration
content:
Hi Climbo Team,
I hope this message finds you well.
I’d like to share some feature requests that will enhance usability and user experience, especially for our Japanese-speaking customers and broader Asia-Pacific audience.

1. Localization Request – Date Field Formatting:
Currently, the DATE field is being translated, which results in a format that is difficult to read and looks strange in Japanese. It would be much more user-friendly if the DATE field remains un-translated and is always displayed in the YYYY-MM numeric format. This standardization will improve clarity and consistency across languages.

2. Custom Prompt for Manual Reply Generator AI:
At present, custom prompts can be set for the automatic reply AI, but it appears the feature is only available when the automatic AI is active. I would like to request the ability to set up and use custom prompts for the manual reply generator AI, independent of the automatic reply setting. This flexibility will help us tailor responses more effectively.

3. Integration with LINE Messenger:
LINE is a primary communication tool in Japan, Taiwan, and Thailand, and is widely used globally. Integrating LINE Messenger—similar to WhatsApp or WeChat—will greatly expand our communication capabilities for text chats, voice calls, and video conferences, making our platform more accessible and relevant for millions of users.

Please let me know if you need additional details or clarification on any of these points. I believe these enhancements will significantly improve our product for our international user base.

Thank you for your consideration.

Best regards,
Jose

title: Feature Request: AI Approval Mode / Human Review Workflow
content:
Problem: Right now the AI system is very “all or nothing.”

Either:
[ul][li]The AI is fully automated and posting/responding automaticallyor[li]The AI is completely turned off.

The issue is that many business owners are still skeptical of AI, especially during onboarding and early adoption.

A lot of users are not yet comfortable with fully autonomous posting, replying, or publishing without first seeing what the AI created.

This creates hesitation and reduces trust in activating the AI features.

Requested Feature:Create a middle-ground workflow between:
1.Full Automation
2.Fully Manual

Suggested Solution: Add an “Approval Mode” or “Review Before Posting” setting across the platform’s AI features.

How It Would Work: When enabled, the AI would:
[ol:1][li]Generate the content normally[li]Send a notification to the user[li]Place the content into a “Pending Approval” queue[li]Allow the user to: Approve Edit Reject Schedule
Once approved, the content goes live automatically \(if past schedule date/time\), or as scheduled \(if approved before scheduled date/time\).

User Experience Goal: This gives business owners “training wheels” while they build trust with the AI.

Over time, many users may eventually switch to full autopilot once they consistently see quality output.

Why This Matters: Right now, users feel like they must either:
[ul][li]Hand over complete controlor[li]Not use the AI properly at all.
That creates unnecessary friction.

This feature creates a smoother transition into AI adoption.

How to think about it: It’s like hiring a new employee.
At first, you want to check their work before it goes out to customers.

Once trust is built, you become comfortable letting them operate independently.

Benefits:
[ul][li]Reduces fear and skepticism around AI[li]Increases user trust and adoption[li]Improves onboarding experience[li]Makes AI feel collaborative instead of risky[li]Gives users confidence while learning the system[li]Creates a smoother path toward full automation later

title: This Week’s Development Plan
content:
Hi Climbers,

Here’s a quick update on what we’re focusing on this week.

AI Billing

This week we’re rolling out AI billing, a system designed to keep Climbo sustainable and predictable in the long term. Every feature that consumes AI tokens, whether ongoing features like AI agents and AI chat, or features used once per month like sentiment analysis or AI ranking, is now billed directly via your agency balance, allowing us to cover the cost we pay to AI tech partners like OpenAI and ensure we do not incur losses on these features.

Here’s how it works:

You can set a monthly AI token limit per plan, just like you can set monthly messaging limits.

The price per AI token charged via your agency balance is fixed, which means you know the exact maximum spending for each plan. This allows you to price your plans confidently, knowing the minimum profit you will generate, and ensures long-term sustainability for your business.

You’ll be able to see exactly how many tokens each client is consuming, and which AI features are using those tokens.

Every AI feature can be enabled or disabled per custom plan, giving you full control over what your clients can access.

If a client reaches their monthly token limit, ongoing AI features like agents and AI chat will pause until the start of the next month when their tokens are refreshed.

Our goal here is not to profit from AI tokens, we want to make sure Climbo remains sustainable and predictable for the long term, even as AI usage grows across all our clients.

Work in Progress: Agency Team Features & Client Integrations

Alongside AI billing, we’re beginning work on agency team features. These will allow your team members to collaborate in Climbo without seeing the underlying platform branding, helping you run a professional SaaS operation with full team management.

We’re also advancing client-side integrations with major platforms including Apple Maps, TikTok, and LinkedIn. These integrations will streamline workflows, simplify automation, and allow your clients to access more powerful functionality directly from their Climbo dashboard.

content:
I have just had a review experience from another company which made me think whether we could have this as an option? That is compliant with the filtering with Google.

So I received an email from them and on the bottom of the email, it simply says ' Are you satisfied with our service? Gives on the email in Green 'Yes Fully' and in Red - 'No, not at all - my request must be escalated. \(I had the option of not doing anything either and just ignore.\)

I pressed green, it took me to another page, it gave me the option to give comments or not. I didn't, I just pressed send. Then I received an email 'inviting' me whether I wanted to share my experience with Trust Pilot.

I just thought I would share, as I feel like this would be a good option for those clients wishing to compliantly filter?

title: FR: SEO Agent replies set to draft, email notify & human approval
content:
Would anyone find this as a helpful feature?

The SEO Agent would auto reply to any reviews but keep them in draft ready for human approval by the client.

We can then let our clients feel the full effect of the AI replies without worrying that they will be weird and horrible etc, then once they review a couple manually they have the option to turn on full auto mode once they are satisified with the replies etc.

Some client may want to always keep the auto replies set to draft so they can manually edit and approve so there should be email notifications ideally too for each reply generated ready for approval.

To improve on that would be to have the review text and reply text in the email with a confirm link/button in the email for even easier/quicker approval by the client.

Thoughts?

title: Which blog setup works better for SEO & GEO? 👀
content:
Hey everyone, I’d love to get your thoughts on something SEO/GEO-related.

We currently have our blog running on [blog.domain.com](http://blog.domain.com), but I’ve been researching whether [domain.com/blog](http://domain.com/blog) would perform better for SEO and GEO \(AI search visibility\).

From what I understand:

[domain.com/blog](http://domain.com/blog) \(subfolder\) usually performs better for SEO/GEO than [blog.domain.com](http://blog.domain.com) \(subdomain\) because Google often treats a subdomain as a more separate website.

Why this matters:

Shared authority → [domain.com/blog](http://domain.com/blog) benefits directly from the main domain’s backlinks, trust, and SEO strength.

Stronger internal linking → blog posts, product pages, and landing pages reinforce each other on the same domain.

Faster indexing & crawling → Google already crawls the main site regularly, so new content may get discovered faster.

Better topical authority → all content contributes to one SEO/GEO ecosystem instead of splitting signals across multiple properties.

In simple terms:

[domain.com/blog](http://domain.com/blog) = one strong [websiteblog.domain.com](http://websiteblog.domain.com) = almost like running a second website in Google’s eyes.

Our challenge is that we can create [www.domain.com/blog](http://www.domain.com/blog) and embed the blog widget there, but when someone opens an article, they still get redirected to [blog.domain.com](http://blog.domain.com).

How do you see this? Would that setup still give meaningful SEO/GEO benefits, or does the redirect basically remove most of the advantage of having /blog on the main domain?

Curious how others think about this and what has worked in practice.

title: Dashboard Customization: how to hide specific sections/tabs?
content:
Hi everyone!

I have a rather unique use case and wanted to brain-storm with the dev-minded or more technical agencies here.

Unlike the standard model, I would use Climbo as a "backend" for my own proprietary app \(which already handles message delivery\). Thanks to everyone and have a great day!

Climbo Team 🫡

title: 🌟 Coming Tomorrow: Branded AI Agents & AI Chat – AMA Recording (21/05/2026)
content:
Hi Climbers!

Today we had an exciting AMA where we presented the next big update to your AI experience! The new features will be released tomorrow. Here’s a preview of what’s coming:

Your Branded AI Agents

You can now fully brand each AI agent by setting a photo and a name. Think of them as your virtual team members—your clients can see three distinct AI employees on your website and platform, making the AI feel personal, professional, and aligned with your brand. This is perfect for giving your SaaS a polished, client-facing look while keeping everything fully white-labeled.

Your AI Chat – Powered by Climbo MCP

The AI chat allows your clients to interact directly with your branded AI to perform tasks they would normally do manually. This feature is in beta, so it’s a perfect time to try it and provide feedback.

Clients can ask the AI chat for things like:

Adding contacts and creating review request templates automatically.
Sending review requests via email, SMS, or WhatsApp.
Updating Google Business Profile \(GBP\) information, like special hours, descriptions, or categories.
Analyzing reviews and private feedback to summarize sentiment or trends.
Tracking which reviews mention specific employees or words.

Basically, anything available via the API can be done by simply asking the AI in every language. This makes tasks fast, simple, and accessible even for non-technical clients.

Watch Live Examples

During today’s AMA, we demonstrated the AI chat in action, showing how simple questions can automate real client tasks. Make sure to watch the AMA recording to see live examples.

Notes
[ul][li]The AI chat is free for the initial launch.[li]In the future, it will be a paid feature via your balance, charging per token used \(input/output\) based on OpenAI pricing.[li]This is part of a broader push to bring AI automation under your brand, providing your clients with a seamless, professional, and efficient experience.

Welcome your clients to a next-level branded AI experience that simplifies managing reviews, content, and business updates—all through a chat interface that feels natural and human.

title: Feature Request: Guided Review Prompts for Semantic SEO (Problem-Solution-Result-Location)
content:
Hi [@Giacomo Chinellato](obj://user/edfd679f109c4ef4b46645bd3d030dcf),
Since Google and AI search engines now prioritize review semantics over raw star ratings and meaningless reviews, could we get a feature that adds customizable guided prompts to the review collection funnel?

Lazy customers often leave empty 5-star ratings. I’d love a setting that shows a subtle prompt caption above the text box when they rate us, nudging them to mention:
[ul][li]The Problem they had.[li]The Solution/Service we provided.[li]The Result they got.[li]The Location \(City/Town\).

Here is a quick example of what I mean:
[ul][li]Before \(Lazy Client\): ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ "Great job, thanks!"[li]After \(Guided by Prompt\): ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ "Our AC broke down \(Problem\) during a heatwave here in Austin \(Location\). They came out the same day for an emergency repair \(Solution\), and now our house is ice-cold and running perfectly again \(Result\)!"

Why it matters: This naturally encourages high-value, long-tail local SEO keywords into their reviews without violating Google's guidelines against forced scripting, helping our agency clients rank significantly better on Google Maps and AI search.

Would love to see this on the roadmap!
Thanks,

title: Workaround Feature Request: Compliant Sentiment Routing & Customer Recovery Flow
content:
One of the biggest recent challenges for reputation management agencies after Google’s updated review policies is maintaining high review conversion while staying fully compliant with anti-review-gating rules.

Current issue: Previously, many agencies used “star wall” style flows where unhappy customers were redirected to private feedback while happy customers were guided toward public reviews. Since Google now restricts this behavior, agencies need a compliant alternative that still helps businesses recover negative experiences before they become public complaints.

Suggested feature: Introduce a “Compliant Sentiment Routing” system instead of review filtering.
Suggested flow example:
[ol:1][li]Customer scans QR code[li]Customer selects experience sentiment \(emoji, stars, slider, etc.\)[li]Both options ALWAYS remain available:[li]Based on sentiment selection, dynamically adjust:
Example: Positive sentiment:
[ul][li]Emphasize “Leave a Google Review.”[li]Show celebratory/review-focused copy
Negative sentiment:
[ul][li]Emphasize “Contact Support” or “Send Feedback.”[li]Trigger instant alerts to the business[li]Still keep Google review access visible and accessible
Why this helps:
[ul][li]Keeps agencies and businesses compliant with Google policies[li]Preserves customer recovery workflows[li]Improves customer support response times[li]Helps businesses resolve issues before escalation[li]Creates additional SaaS value beyond simple review collection

This would help position the platform as a true reputation management and customer recovery tool rather than just a review collection tool. What do you think?

title: 🍏 Apple is Ramping Up its Business Platform - to rival GBP!
content:
APPLE BUSINESS

Starting April 14, Apple will switch to a more GBP-Like business platform with manager access and other features. It will no longer be called Apple Business Connect but "Apple Business". And it will also include an Apple Business Manager, similar to the Google Business Profile Manager.

Why now? Apple wants to boost its bottom line with a new ads platform. Which is why they want to improve user experience for Apple Business users.

Here is what you can expect, some of them will be new, others are just pulled from the currently existing Apple Business Connect platform.

New brand profiles: Manage brand name, logo, and key details consistently across Apple Maps, Wallet, and other features and apps.

Place cards: Customize with photos, detailed location information, hours, and other useful details that display across Apple Maps, Safari, Spotlight, and more.

Showcases and custom actions: Highlight deals, special offers, new products, or seasonal items on place cards in Maps. Add custom actions like order or reserve to direct customers to a preferred website or app.

Location insights: Gain valuable insights into how customers discover and interact with businesses on Maps, including search, views, and taps on actions.

Branded communications: Display branding prominently in the Mail app and on iCloud Mail to increase awareness. Branding will display with tracked orders in Wallet for a more recognizable customer experience.

Tap to Pay on iPhone: Build trust by displaying a brand logo and name on the payment screen when accepting payments directly on iPhone.

Apple Maps ads are coming in Summer to US and Canada

Apple Business Essentials, Apple Business Manager, and Apple Business Connect will no longer be available once Apple Business launches.

=======================================

Surely this must be good for us, as it will mean more people wanting to get reviews for their businesses

What are your thoughts ?

title: Important Update: Google Review Policy Compliance
content:
Hey Climbers,

Over the last few days, there has been a lot of discussion around Google’s review policies, especially around review gating, employee mentions, incentives, QR codes, and how review requests should be handled.

We have reviewed the policy carefully, and we want to make sure Climbo continues to help businesses collect genuine customer feedback in the safest and most compliant way possible.

Google’s policy is very clear on a few key points:

“Contributions to Google Maps should reflect a genuine experience at a place or business.”

Google also states that merchants must not:

“Discourage or prohibit negative reviews, or selectively solicit positive reviews from customers.”

And when soliciting reviews, merchants should not:

“Require or pressure users to leave ratings or write reviews while on the premises, nor should they request that specific content be included.”

This includes:

“Merchants requesting that staff solicit reviews that include specific content, including content that identifies a staff member.”

At the same time, Google also clearly says that merchants are allowed to:

“Solicit or encourage the posting of content that does represent a genuine experience, without offering incentives to do so or attempting to influence the rating or the contents of the review.”

So the goal is not to stop collecting reviews.

The goal is to collect reviews in the right way: neutral, genuine, non-selective, and without incentives or pressure.

Here is what we are changing.

1. Review Gating: Removing Sentiment-Based Filters

We are removing review filtering based on sentiment.

This means we will no longer support flows where the customer is first asked to choose a rating, stars, thumbs up/down, smiley face, or any other sentiment indicator, and then receives a different experience based on that response.

For example, this type of flow is no longer considered safe:

Happy customer → sent to Google
Unhappy customer → sent to private feedback form

That can be interpreted as selectively soliciting positive reviews and discouraging negative reviews.

title: This Week’s Development Plan
content:
Hi Climbers,

here’s what we’re focusing on this week.

One of the biggest improvements is that Agency Mode will become accessible directly under your own domain, without the need for a CNAME configuration.

The long-term goal behind this change is much bigger than just convenience. In the future, when we introduce more advanced team members and sales team features, your collaborators will access Agency Mode directly from your own branded domain, without ever seeing Climbo branding or infrastructure behind the scenes. This is an important step toward making the platform feel even more like a completely independent SaaS under your brand.

We’re also happy to say that the Google Sheets integration has finally been approved by Google and is now ready for release. This will allow businesses to automatically import contacts directly from spreadsheets without relying on external automations.

Another major focus this week is the beginning of the Climbo MCP development. We’re starting to build the infrastructure that will allow AI systems and external agents to interact with Climbo in a much more advanced and structured way. More details on this soon.

We’ll also release the new review link compliant experience, based on all the discussions and feedback around Google policies. The goal is to create an experience where customers can always easily choose between leaving a public review or private feedback, while keeping the flow optimized for conversion and fully compliant.

More updates soon, and as always thank you for all the feedback you keep sharing in the community.

content:
Hi Climbers 👋

Here’s what we’ll be building and refining this week.

You’ll be able to choose the order of platforms \(Google, Facebook, Trustpilot, etc.\) shown to the end customer, so you can prioritize the platform that matters most for your client’s visibility.

title: Important Compliance Update for Our Website Review Widgets
content:
As we conduct our full compliance clean up, one significant area remains incomplete.

The website review widgets we currently use are not compliant.

Because we filter these widgets to show only four and five star ratings, we must make several updates to meet strict transparency guidelines.

We must clearly label the widget section to reflect that the content is curated. We need to use titles like Featured Testimonials or Selected Highlights instead of a generic Customer Reviews label .

We must also provide a highly visible link immediately next to the widget. This link must allow visitors to access and read all of our unedited and unfiltered reviews.

title: AMA Replay: AI Ranking Analysis, GEO Agent Updates & More
content:
Hi Climbers,

the recording of yesterday’s AMA is now available.

We covered several important updates and upcoming features, including one of the biggest additions we’ve ever worked on for the Visibility section.

We started with the new Google policy updates and the changes we’re implementing on our side to keep the platform aligned and compliant moving forward.

We also presented the new GEO Agent topics editor, which will allow users to view the topics the agent uses to generate blog articles and have more control over the content strategy.

Another important update: the latest articles generated by the GEO Agent can now be automatically embedded directly inside the Climbo pre-built websites, helping businesses keep their websites constantly updated with fresh localized content.

Then we showed the new AI Ranking Analysis, which is one of the biggest feature we developed.

Your clients will be able to see where their business ranks inside AI-generated answers on platforms like:

ChatGPT
Gemini
Perplexity
Grok

For prompts such as:

“Suggest me the best dentist in Miami”

But the most important part is not just the ranking itself — it’s understanding the reason behind that ranking.

For example:

“You rank lower on ChatGPT because your competitors have a stronger presence on TripAdvisor and more review mentions around specific keywords.”

This will help local businesses finally understand how AI platforms perceive them, why competitors are ranking above or below them, and what actions they should take to improve visibility inside LLM-generated answers.

We also shared ongoing updates regarding:

Google Sheets integration
Apple Maps integration
MCP-related developments

Thanks again to everyone who joined live and contributed with questions and feedback. More big updates coming soon.

• discoverability

The more I think about it, the more it feels less like a traditional website and more like a:
“Dynamic Local Presence Profile Hub” with all its fast indexing and SEO ranking ability.

Almost like a LinkedIn Profile for local businesses.

LinkedIn profiles are structured professional identity layers for:

• reputation
• credibility

• updates
• discoverability

…and this system is starting to feel very similar, but for local businesses. Which is powerful!

Especially because the business actually owns it on their own domain, instead of renting visibility inside another platform.

It almost becomes like a supercharged business card that:

• gets found in Google
• strengthens AI/search discoverability
• continuously updates from GBP
• feels part of the business ecosystem
• supports the existing main website instead of replacing it

title: Utilising the Opt In Feature in light of Google's Policy
content:
A quick clarification and some thoughts around Google reviews and incentives 👇

It says you cannot offer discounts or rewards in exchange for a Google review, as this goes against Google’s review policies.

However, as part of normal business practice, what you can do is encourage customers to join an email or VIP list for exclusive promotions, updates, and special offers.

Then, as part of their normal customer communication, they can later follow up and ask for feedback or a Google review — as long as the offer was for joining the mailing list itself, not for leaving a review and no incentive for leaving the review is presented.

Example flow:

✅ Customer joins your VIP list for offers "Join our VIP list for member-only offers & updates"
✅ You send promotions to all subscribers
✅ You later ask customers for honest feedback or a review \(no mention of incentive\)

The important part it seems is keeping the review request messaging clear about the 'review only'
The mailing list that may or may not have offers down the road is to all of their list.

This way they have a customer, they can continue to:

[ul][li]market to[li]and send future review requests to

I always wondered about the positioning of the 'Opt In' feature...
However its use case from the platform may be even more useful?

Maybe the tick box text being able to be amended to something like this may be useful:

☐ I agree to receive updates, offers, and feedback requests from \[Business Name\]. I can opt out anytime.

Thoughts? Compliant?

title: April Google Polices 2026
content:
The most critical changes for April 2026 include:
[ul][li]Employee Name Solicitation Prohibited: Reviews should no longer contain or solicit specific staff or employee names.[li]"Review Gating" Ban: You cannot specifically ask only happy customers for reviews while directing unhappy ones to a private feedback form. This is considered manipulation.[li]Incentive Restrictions: Offering incentives, including employee-level bonuses specifically for receiving 5-star reviews, can trigger review removal.[li]No "Review Templates": Pushing a specific script or template for customers to follow is prohibited.[li]School Review Disabling: As of late April 2025 \(continuing into 2026\), Google has disabled reviews for general education institutions \(K-12 schools\), removing existing ratings to protect against misleading content.
Key Takeaways for Businesses:
[ul][li]Avoid on-site review prompts: Do not ask for reviews while the customer is on the premises, as this can be flagged as forced or incentivized.[li]Automated Deletions: AI is actively filtering reviews that break these rules, resulting in sudden drops in review counts.[li]Focus on Organic Content: Encourage, "Share your genuine experience," rather than asking for specific keywords or names.

title: This Week’s Plan: LLMs Ranking Analytics + GEO Agent Improvements
content:
Hi Climbers,

this week we’re focused on closing the loop on Client Mode, by building one of the most requested features on the roadmap: LLMs Ranking Analytics, inside the Visibility section.

The goal is to allow your clients to track their visibility in answers generated by LLMs for local prompts like:

“Suggest me the best \[category\] in \[city\]”

We will track rankings across multiple LLMs, including ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, Perplexity, and Claude. For each platform, the user will be able to see their position and, most importantly, the reason behind that position.

For example:

“You are ranked #8 on ChatGPT because you don’t stand out enough on TripAdvisor. We recommend getting more reviews on TripAdvisor to improve your ranking.”

This is key because each LLM evaluates different signals and pulls data from different sources: Google, TripAdvisor, Yelp, websites, local content, reviews, mentions, authority, and more. With this feature, local business owners will finally understand not just where they rank, but also why competitors are ranked higher or lower than them.

At the same time, we’ll continue improving the GEO Agent based on your feedback. In particular, we’ll work on giving users the ability to view the topics the agent uses to generate articles, and if possible, also edit and add custom topics.

We’ll also improve the images generated for articles by upgrading to the new OpenAI Image 2.0 model, which is significantly more powerful and produces higher-quality visuals.

As always, we’ll share progress during the next AMA.

This week is a big step toward making the Visibility section even more powerful, actionable, and valuable for your clients.

Also, today — as every first Monday of the month — we have the Sales Accelerator live with Wayne Connell. Add it to your agenda here:

[https://www.skool.com/climbo/calendar?eid=ebdd586679584830bb206df7adc60bde&eoid=1777924800](https://www.skool.com/climbo/calendar?eid=ebdd586679584830bb206df7adc60bde&eoid=1777924800)
