title: 💡 Feature Idea: Showing Review Views Inside the Climbo Dashboard
content:
Hey everyone,
[@Giacomo Chinellato](obj://user/edfd679f109c4ef4b46645bd3d030dcf)

During the skills/sales training with Wayne, he showed a really interesting trick that I didn’t know about before.

If a Google account is set to public, you can actually see how many views a review has received on a business profile.

Most people never notice this, but when you check it, many reviews easily get 1,000+ views \(sometimes much more\). That’s a huge amount of visibility and social proof.

That got me thinking:

Would it be possible \(if technically feasible\) to integrate this into the Climbo dashboard?

For example, under Reviews, where we already see all incoming reviews, it could show per review:

– Reviewer name
– Review content
– And underneath: number of views \(refreshed monthly where available\)

Even if it only updates once per month, this would already add a lot of value.
It would:

• Make the real exposure of reviews visible to clients
• Strengthen the perceived ROI of reputation management
• Show that reviews do much more than just collect star ratings

I’m not sure how complex this would be to implement \(since it depends on public profiles\), but if it’s possible at all, I think this would be a very cool and high-value feature.

Curious to hear what everyone thinks!

title: Google Policy Update
content:
I think it's a good thing and I'm sure we can all evolve clients away from the review gating era, I have looked at a lot of GBP on maps in recent weeks and there is a need to educate business owners who maybe blissfully unaware of the money they are leaving on the table by not utilising the free GBP they have. So it is our mission to educate the business owners to get them to notice and then offer them the solution we have to help them maximise on this awesome local search resource.

No one wants to see a 5 star listed business with no bad reviews, that just screams dodgy, in an ideal world and with the use of AI the real true rankings can come out, even a poor review handle and replied to promptly and professionally will go a long way to influencing the google AI algorithm.

Pictures and updates, due to the use of AI Picture scans it's also a handy way to get a conversation going with a potential client, if you notice a picture on the profile that captures an offer for a free drink or some incentive for a review to inform them to remove this as it will be cause a potential suspension.

I feel that we are now not just involved in Reputation management but also Suspension Avoidance too.
Have a great week Climbers

JL

title: Turn bookings into instant review opportunities
content:
Our software is primarily aimed at local business owners and small businesses, many of which rely on or benefit from reservations \(restaurants, dentists, etc.\). So why not introduce a way to collect contact information when making a booking? This would be similar to our existing opt-in system, but enhanced to include full name, email, phone number, number of people and appointment time.

People are often hesitant to share their information just to receive a review request, but they’re much more willing to provide it to secure a reservation. By doing this, we not only obtain the customer’s contact details but also know the exact time of their visit - allowing us to send a review request shortly afterward, while the positive experience is still fresh in their mind.

title: Objection Handling
content:
I thought I’d share this if it helps anyone and would love to hear how you handle your objections?

Objection Handling

They: “We already get reviews”.

You:
“That’s great — that means your customers are happy.
What we do is turn those into a consistent visibility engine rather than occasional feedback.”

Pause.

“Would you like to see how that looks for your business?”

That’s it.

No over-explaining.
No defending.

Then: “Let’s activate it for 14 days and measure what happens.”

———————-

They “We’re already busy”.

You smile and say:

“That’s brilliant, which means you’ve built something worth protecting”.

Pause.

Let that land.

Then continue: “That’s exactly when you should put it in place, it ensures it stays that way”.

“This isn’t about getting busier —
it’s about protecting the position you’ve built.”

“Busy businesses still lose enquiries to competitors with stronger visibility.”

“This just makes sure no competitor quietly overtakes you while you’re focused on the work.”

“It protects your visibility, strengthens your reputation, and keeps you positioned — so you don’t feel dips before they happen.”

“Shall we switch it on and let the system show you on a free trial. No card. No catch?”

🔥 The Core Objections You’ll Face

1️⃣ “We’re already busy.”

We covered this.

Translation:
“I don’t see urgency.”

Response category:
Protection & Positioning.

⸻

2️⃣ “We already get reviews.”

Translation:
“I don’t see the gap.”

Response category:
Leverage & Consistency.

Reframe:
“It’s not about getting some reviews — it’s about controlling momentum.”

⸻

3️⃣ “How much time will this take?”

Translation:
“I don’t want another system.”

Response category:
Ease & Automation.

Power response:
“You don’t manage it — it runs. We set it up, it works in the background.”

Short. Clean.

⸻

4️⃣ “We don’t really use social media.”

Translation:
“I don’t understand amplification.”

Response category:
Visibility beyond social.

Reframe:
“That’s fine — the system strengthens your Google presence first. Social is just bonus authority.”

title: Making Requests Simple — a small but meaningful UI change
content:
We’ve been listening to you, agency owners who run the platform day-to-day, and the feedback is clear.

Today the Reviews section lets you send a quick request via email/SMS, but you can’t pick a saved template or send WhatsApp, while Requests handles campaigns, but only to lists. That can create a “where do I send this from?” moment for clients and extra support work for you.

We want to fix that by making Reviews only for reading and replying to reviews, and by moving every sending action into Requests. From Requests, you’ll be able to send a campaign to lists, or a quick request to a single contact, and in both cases, you just pick a template and go. No last-minute text edits during the flow: pick the template, send, done. The goal is a single place where your clients go to ask for reviews.

We’re also simplifying auto campaigns. The current frequency/time model confuses people. The new approach is: enable the auto campaign, pick a delay \(for example, 10 minutes\), and choose the template. Any new contact gets the request after that delay.

These changes come directly from your AMA comments and support tickets. We want the platform to be something you can hand to a local business owner and have them use without a long explanation.

Tell us what you think: your input shapes the next iteration! 🫡

title: Proposal to Enhance the Review Collection System
content:
Dear Team,
I would like to propose an improvement to the review request form that could significantly increase the strategic value of the data collected.

The idea is to allow users to add customizable evaluation criteria \(for example, for a bed and breakfast: cleanliness, breakfast quality, comfort, overall aesthetics\), with a 1 to 5 star rating for each parameter, without requiring integration with external review platforms.

This would make it possible to:
[ul][li]Obtain more detailed and targeted feedback.[li]Clearly identify strengths and areas for improvement.[li]Maintain direct control over perceived service quality.[li]Generate high-value proprietary data for analysis.[li]
Additionally, the ability to export this data in CSV format or access it via API would allow integration with analytics tools, CRM systems, or business intelligence platforms, further increasing the strategic usefulness of the solution.

I believe this enhancement could represent an important differentiating factor and added value for users.
Thank you for your attention and for your continued development efforts.

title: Handling URL changes for QR/NFC when migrating to Climbo 2.0
content:
Hi everyone!
I’m planning my migration from Climbo Classic to Climbo 2.0, but I’ve run into a technical challenge regarding my existing links.

I have many QR codes, NFC chips, and printed materials already in the field using my current URL structure. Since I want to avoid replacing all my physical marketing materials, I need to ensure the old links redirect to the new 2.0 structure.

The catch: My subdomains are hosted externally via CloudFront \(pointing to a CloudFront address\). This means I cannot use a standard .htaccess file on my web host to manage these redirects, as DNS alone doesn't handle path-level redirects.

My questions for the community:
[ul][li]For those who migrated, did Climbo 2.0 provide an internal way to "map" or redirect old Classic URLs to the new ones?

I’m curious if there is a "Climbo-native" way to handle this.
Thanks for sharing your experiences!

title: 📍 Build Local First: The Real Climbo Advantage
content:
Nice comment from [@Nilo Cere](obj://user/a7eb0cf7e45b4f218d5b247390e9a179), this is the mindset that actually works.

Focus locally: meet owners, understand the market, build trust face-to-face, and make your first demos visible. Start small, test your pitch, iterate quickly, and accept the mistakes: those early experiments teach you more than any overnight growth hack. Small wins \(a demo, a trial, a first paying client\) compound over time and become predictable MRR and strong referrals.

It’s a long game and a learning process. Keep studying the lessons, keep talking to prospects, and keep improving your system. The real value you build isn’t only clients, it’s skill. And skill makes the business inevitable.

title: 🚀 This Week’s Development Focus — ⚠️ Website Update!
content:
Hello Climbers,

Here’s what we’re working on this week.

1️⃣ Finishing the Website Feature

We’re finalizing the new website feature — and there’s an important change that comes directly from your feedback.

Instead of keeping the website as a sub-feature inside the Profile section, we’ve decided to make it one of the main platform features \(it will become the 6th core feature\). It will be activable/deactivable from your Agency custom plans.

This gives you full flexibility.

If you want to keep your offer highly vertical and focused \(for example, reviews-only\), you can simply deactivate the website feature from your custom plans.

If you want to include it as part of a higher-tier package, bundle it, or even sell it separately — you can do that too.

So if you're pitching a business that already has an established website, you can offer a plan that does not include the website feature.

If you're pitching a business that doesn’t have a website yet, you can include it in the plan, increase the perceived value, and charge accordingly.

This way, everyone wins — flexibility at the agency level.

2️⃣ Instant Google Reviews Import + Instant Notifications

We’ve figured out how to properly work with Google webhooks, and we’re building a system that will:

• Instantly import Google reviews as soon as they are left by end customers
• Trigger an email notification when a new review is imported
• Trigger a push notification for those who have the mobile app

This means no more delays — real-time visibility.

End customers leave a review → it appears immediately inside the platform → your clients get notified instantly.

This will massively improve perceived value and engagement.

We’ll keep pushing hard this week.
As always, thank you for the feedback — it’s shaping the product in real time.

Let’s build. 💪

title: 🚀 This Week’s Feature Release — A.M.A. Recording (12/02/2026)
content:
Hi Climbers,

This week we shipped two major updates that a lot of you have been asking for, and they’re both designed to make the platform feel more automatic for your clients, while giving you more control as an agency.

First: your clients can now generate a fully optimized website just by connecting their Google Business Profile via login. The site is built automatically using their real business data, and from inside the platform they can quickly tailor it — turning sections on/off, adjusting the color theme, and connecting a custom domain. We built this with performance and SEO as a priority: it’s extremely fast, structured with proper rich snippets, and optimized not only for Google, but also for LLM visibility \(AI search systems\).

And because we know every agency positions things differently, Agency Mode lets you control how this feature is offered. You can enable or disable the website feature directly in your custom plans, keep it off if you want to stay vertically focused, include it in premium tiers, or sell it as a separate upgrade. You decide how it fits your offer.

Second: we’ve launched instant Google review import plus real-time notifications. If a client has connected Google via login, new reviews are now imported immediately as soon as they’re published — no delays. The moment a review goes live, it shows up inside the platform, an email notification is sent, and a push notification is triggered for clients using the mobile app. The result is a much more real-time experience that feels “alive,” keeps clients engaged, and increases perceived value.

This release is planned for tomorrow, Friday, February 13. We’d love your feedback on both. Try them, play with how you position them in your plans, and let us know what you think 💪

title: This week’s development focus 👇
content:
We’re continuing with our weekly sprint, and here’s what we’ll be working on in the coming days:

Tiered Pricing
We’re introducing location-based pricing tiers, so you can create Stripe plans like:

Bronze for 1–3 locations
Silver for 4–10 locations
Gold for 11–20 locations

This will give you more flexibility in how you package and sell Climbo to multi-location clients.

Coupon Codes
You’ll be able to create coupon codes that your clients can apply directly at Stripe checkout. This will make it easier to run promotions, offer discounts, or create special deals when needed.

White-Label Mobile App \(iOS & Android\)
We’ll continue working on the white-label mobile app service, which allows you to have your own branded app published on the App Store and Play Store. This remains a paid, done-for-you service for agencies that want to take retention and brand authority to the next level.

You can follow progress and see what’s coming next on the roadmap here:
👉 [https://roadmap.climbo.com](https://roadmap.climbo.com)

We’ll walk through everything during Thursday’s Weekly AMA, with a live demo of new features and an open Q&A.
To join, go to the Calendar, select the event, and add it to your agenda so you don’t miss it.

See you live.

title: Setting up review flows for multiple real estate BVs – concerned about negative reviews, experiences?
content:
Hey everyone,
I’m about to set up review flows for multiple real estate BVs and I have to admit, it feels a bit risky.
Here’s the situation:
• One BV claimed their Trustpilot profile in the past• Actively requested reviews for a short period• Then stopped managing it• Result: a lot of negative reviews and now a 3.1 rating
The other BVs:
• Never claimed their Trustpilot profiles• 0 reviews• Even though they buy/sell 30+ properties per month• 1000+ leads per month through ads
My assumption is that because nothing was actively opened or pushed on Trustpilot, people were less likely to leave reviews.
Now I’m considering properly setting everything up and implementing a structured review flow.
But here’s my concern:
If I claim and activate these profiles, they become more [visible.In](http://visible.In) real estate, there will always be people who don’t move forward or feel disappointed.
Questions for those working in similar high-volume industries:
[ol:1][li]Do you have experience running review flows in real estate?[li]Does the review filter actually work well in this industry?[li]Have you ever seen a wave of negative reviews after activating Trustpilot?[li]Do most negative signals truly get captured in the private feedback filter before going public?
We want to build a structured, professional system.But I don’t want to unintentionally “wake up” something that could damage the reputation.
Would really appreciate insights, especially from anyone handling high lead volumes like this.

title: 🤝 Community Rules — Read Before Posting/Commenting
content:
Our goal is to create an environment that is positive, collaborative, and focused on meaningful contribution. This is not simply a forum; it is a shared space where agency owners, marketers, and entrepreneurs support each other in building long-term software businesses. To maintain a constructive and professional atmosphere, we ask that all members follow the guidelines outlined below.

1. Identification
We ask every member to maintain a complete Skool profile, including a real first and last name, a profile photo, and a short personal bio. We are building a community of professionals, and openness is essential in creating trust, credibility, and genuine collaboration.

2. High-Quality Contributions
Before posting, consider whether what you are sharing adds value to the community. Use clear and descriptive titles, explain the context behind your question or insight, and choose the appropriate category. Aim to make your contribution helpful to others, not just yourself. When everyone contributes thoughtfully, the community becomes more useful, more supportive, and more inspiring.

3. No Account-Specific Support
General questions that can benefit the wider community should be posted in the Support category. However, issues related to the white-label setup, billing, login, or technical issues or bugs should not be posted in the community. These must be handled through a support ticket inside the Climbo platform \(Help → Support\), which allows our team to properly track, prioritize, and resolve your request. This community supports Climbo 2.0 only. Questions related to Climbo 1.0 are not supported in the community forum. If you still have questions about Climbo 1.0, please handle them privately through a support ticket and not via public community posts. Please do not contact the Climbo team through private messages or external channels for support requests. Always use the support ticket system or the community in the appropriate sections to ensure clarity and fairness for everyone.

title: Feature Request: Critical Alert for Google Business Profile Disconnection
content:
Hi everyone,
We need to talk about a "silent killer" : Google Business Profiles getting disconnected without any warning.

Whether it's an API token expiring or a Google security refresh, the platform stays silent.

We only find out when it’s too late—usually when a frustrated client calls to complain.

The Solution is simple: We need an automated Notification System \(Email/SMS/Webhook\) that alerts the Admin the second a location's status flips from "Connected" to "Disconnected."

We can't be expected to manually log into every single dashboard daily just to check if a toggle is still green.

I’ve already submitted this as a Feature Request, but we need to push this to the top of the roadmap.
If this has happened to you, drop a "+" or a comment below.

Let’s show the team that this is a priority! 👇

title: Question about language handling in Review Flow & SEO Agent 🌍
content:
I know that for each client the platform can be delivered in a different language \(for example English or Spanish\), or that they can adjust this themselves in the settings. That part works well.

Question 1 – Review flow / QR / opt-inWhen scanning the QR code or using the opt-in page, I notice that the default question \(e.g. “How would you rate your overall experience with us?”\) stays in my own [language.Is](http://language.Is) it correct that this text is automatically shown in the language of the end user’s phone?And what happens if the default text is manually edited \(for example set to English\)? Will it still automatically translate for users with a different phone language, or only when the default text is left unchanged?

Question 2 – SEO AgentIn other features, language detection can be enabled, but for the SEO Agent I only see the option to select one response language.Does this mean that if the SEO Agent is set to Dutch and an English review is received, the reply will be posted in Dutch?And how does this work with keywords: are keywords automatically translated, or are they only used in the selected language?
Curious to understand how this works for clients receiving reviews in multiple languages.

title: QR Codes - Employee Tracking
content:
[@Giacomo Chinellato](obj://user/edfd679f109c4ef4b46645bd3d030dcf), I saw this planned feature in the roadmap - QR Codes - Employee Tracking

How are you planning to let it work?

Why I'm asking is because I sell consciously NFC products for the last 4 years and due to trial and error I don't share QR on the products.

The reasons are a few.

[ul][li]The NFC products I use are made of real solid material and the NFC is incorporated into the material. "Unbreakable" for normal use on counters, tables, on the wall, toilets, etc.[li]With QR, if the qr has a scratch on it it will not work anymore. So much more vulnerable.[li]Printing out a qr code and for instance laminate it looks cheap \(and is vulnerable\). If my brand is on a product I don't want to waste my time on stuff that doesn't work \(for a reasonable time\). In 3 years I never had one single NFC product send pack. Program it and it works forever. 

I ask a lot of business owners if they know what NFC is. Most of the time they don't know. If I ask them if the pay their groceries with their phone the say..Aaaahhh, yes I do.

So my vision and experience is that people see the NFC products on counters, tables, on the wall, toilets etc. and know what they have to do \(also as it is mentioned on the NFC product - see atachment\).

I would suggest that the business has like her own affiliate links within their account so every employee has an unique link connected to the review url link of the business, so the businesses can track individual performance. I suggest you want to keep the qr code as well so you have 2 options.

I know I'm not objective in this case but on the other hand I came up with these solutions by trial and error and know how I can make it as easy as possible for the business owners to get more reviews with these products.

I hope you see it that way as well.

Thanks in advance!

title: Best practice: converting free test clients to paid without rebuilding everything
content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently setting up a new automation structure and want to test it properly with around 10 pilot clients.

My idea is to run a 60-day free test period, where I fully set everything up for the client:
[ul][li]Google account connected[li]Review flows built[li]QR codes active[li]Dashboard fully configuredThe only thing they pay for upfront are the custom review boards I produce for them.

Now I’m running into a structural challenge and I’m curious how others handle this.
The problem:When a free client needs to convert to a paid plan:
[ul][li]QR codes change[li]The setup cannot simply be upgraded[li]I would have to rebuild everything from scratch\(flows, texts, photos, connections, etc.\)

This makes scaling pilots very inefficient, because all the value is already proven, but the technical switch creates friction for both me and the client.
My questions:
[ol:1][li]What is the best way to convert a free test client into a paid client without breaking or recreating the setup?[li]How do you personally structure pilots or free test clients so upgrading is seamless?[li]Is there a reason there isn’t a direct free → paid upgrade path where everything stays intact?[li]Would you recommend:

I’m mainly looking for a scalable and client-friendly way to test new automations without doubling setup work.

Curious how you all approach this.

Thanks in advance 🙌

title: Using opt-in + a monthly giveaway to collect reviews (without heavy automation)
content:
Are you actively using the opt-in feature for your clients?
And if so, in what ways does it work best for you?

I’ve been thinking about this and came up with an idea that might work really well without complex automations.

The concept:
For clients \(for example restaurants\), I create a fully personalized table sign.
On the sign it says something like:

“Join this month’s giveaway
Scan the QR code to see what you can win”

When someone scans the QR code, they land on a simple opt-in form where they leave:
– first name
– last name
– email address

At the top of the form it clearly says:
“Win this week”
For example:
– 4x matcha
– free lunch
– dinner vouchers

After submitting the form:
– they are automatically added to the email flow
– they receive a confirmation email the same day that they are participating in the monthly giveaway

In that same email, the review request is kept very natural and calm, for example:

“It’s important for us to understand what our guests think about our restaurant.
Would you like to leave a short review about your experience?
Your feedback really helps us improve.”

Below that, the review link.

So the flow is:
give something first \(the giveaway\)
clear opt-in
then ask for feedback in a normal, honest way.

I feel like this could work really well because it’s low-friction and doesn’t feel pushy.

Curious to hear your thoughts:
– are you already using the opt-in feature in a similar way?
– do you see any risks or improvements?
– or do you have other ideas that build on this approach?

Looking forward to your input 👀

title: 🚀 This Week’s Development Focus
content:
Hello Climbers,

This week, we’re starting work on something big: the pre-built website for your clients.

We’re building a ready-to-use website structure that your clients can launch instantly. All they need to do is connect their Google Business profile, and the website is automatically generated using their real business data: description, business information, opening hours, photos, reviews, services, attributes, and social links. There’s no manual setup, no copy-pasting, and no technical work required.

But this isn’t just a simple auto-generated website. The entire structure is optimized for LLM discovery, meaning it’s designed to be easily understood, indexed, and surfaced by AI search engines. On top of that, our upcoming GEO Agent will continuously update the website with fresh, relevant content, automatically generating new updates based on incoming reviews. This ensures the site stays dynamic, further strengthening your client’s visibility and improving their ranking across AI-driven search results.

This isn’t just a website. It’s an AI-optimized visibility engine built for the future of local search.

These are not small updates, so development may take more than one week. But as always, on Thursday’s AMA, we’ll show you the progress live and walk you through what’s coming 👀

Alongside this, we’re:
• Fixing minor bugs
• Polishing small UX details
• Releasing a few minor features in the background

And don’t forget 👇

🔥 Today — Wayne’s Sales Accelerator
🕘 9 PM Rome time

If you want to improve conversions and close more deals, be there live for the Q&A.

Big week ahead.
Let’s build. 💪

title: New Features Released + Demo 🚀
content:
Hello Climbers,

Yesterday during the AMA we presented a big batch of updates — both for Clients Mode and Agency Mode — plus new resources for you.

Here’s a quick recap of what’s now live 👇

🧑‍💼 Clients Mode Updates

• Custom AI Prompts – Add additional instructions to your AI agents to better reflect your client’s tone and voice.
• Bulk Reply to Past Google Reviews – With one click, reply to all past Google reviews that don’t have a response \(rate-limited safely\).
• AI Activity Section – A new area where clients can see the full history of tasks completed by AI agents.
• Video on Review Link – Embed a custom video on the review landing page to increase review conversion.
• Widget Review Filters – Choose which review platforms to display in widgets by filtering per source.
• Connect Google During Onboarding – Clients can now connect their GBP during onboarding \(before logging in\), so they enter the platform with sections already populated with Google data.

🏢 Agency Mode Update

• Free Trial Banner – Add a top banner for clients in free trial with a custom message and link \(e.g., upgrade instructions\).

📚 New Resources

• Automations Course – Step-by-step training on building automations for clients using Zapier, n8n, Pabbly, MAke \(inside the Start Here course\).
• Updated Knowledge Base + AI Chatbot – New unbranded articles and chatbot available at the knowledge base. Soon you’ll be able to embed the chatbot directly into your app with one click.

🎥 About the AMA Recording

Unfortunately, the live event wasn’t recorded properly — so I had to remake the demo video this morning.

The good news: below you’ll find the full demo of all the features.

From next week on, I’ll make sure everything is properly recorded so you also get the full Q&A session replay.

Thank you all for the constant feedback inside the community — a lot of these features come directly from your requests.

Have a great weekend.
Next week will be another strong one.

As always, on Monday I’ll post the weekly development goals 🚀

title: Feature request : near real-time review sync
content:
I would like to request a feature:; near‑real‑time notifications when a new Google review is posted to a connected business profile. If true real‑time push is not possible, please implement polling to check for new reviews at intervals no greater than 30 minutes. The current manual refresh and once‑daily synchronization are insufficient.

Why this is important ?

[ul][li]Faster responses: immediate notifications let businesses reply quickly to positive and negative reviews, improving customer relations.[li]Reputation protection: early alerting reduces the impact of negative reviews and speeds escalation for urgent issues.[li]Client value: timely alerts increase perceived value of the platform and client retention.[li]Operational efficiency: reduces manual checking and ensures no new reviews are missed.[li]Competitive parity: many review-management tools offer near‑real‑time alerts; adding this strengthens our offering. 

Important additional options:

[ul][li]Allow notification channels: in‑Climbo , email, and webhook \(so clients can forward to their systems\).[li]Per‑account toggle to enable/disable notifications and configurable frequency for clients who prefer batching.

title: Suggestion: Separate Manual AI Replies from AI Agent Automation (Plan Flexibility in 2.0)
content:
Hey team 👋First of all, really liking the Climbo 2.0 update — the AI agents, automation, and overall flow are a big step up. Great work on this 👍
Gave a demo to few customers and they love it !!
I had one small product/pricing-related question and suggestion.
In 2.0, when the AI Agent is disabled in a plan, it also disables the ability to even manually reply to reviews using AI.
From a packaging perspective, many of us are creating tiered plans, for example:
[ul][li]Basic plan → Manual AI-assisted replies \(limited usage, no automation\)[li]Pro plan → AI Agents enabled \(auto replies + full automation\)
Right now, if AI Agent is OFF, we lose both automation and manual AI replies, which makes it difficult to differentiate plans cleanly.
👉 Suggestion:Would it be possible to separate these two controls?
[ul][li]Manual AI Reply \(can be enabled even when AI Agent is OFF\)[li]AI Agent / Automation \(auto replies, social posting, etc.\)
This would really help in creating a better entry-level plan while keeping automation exclusive to higher plans.
Just sharing feedback — overall, 2.0 is looking really solid 🚀Would love to hear thoughts from the team.

title: I had a colleges feedback on a couple of minor issues
content:
Hello [@Giacomo Chinellato](obj://user/edfd679f109c4ef4b46645bd3d030dcf)

I had a college go through a test free account to see how they went. He found a couple of things I wanted to pass this on to you and has anyone else experienced these. I was able to replicate them when going in.

This is what he said:
"I did see some errors too.. When you update your Full Name, it says "Saved", but there is still nothing there.. Also at the "Teams", it says "No Name", so design needs to look better here.
Also for the Billing section, it doesn't show any pricing.. It will be nice to have the prices clear here"

My findings:
[ol:1][li]I changed the account user name, saved, click on "team", name didn't change, go back to "account" didn't appear to change. I had to refresh the page to the see the change, which isn't really ideal. [li]I found the issue with the NO NAME, In the Account section, the FULL NAME was empty so under 'Teams" it said NO NAME. When I went back and added a name and saved it, I looked in teams again, still didn't update, I returned to the Account, the FULL NAME field was empty. So I thought, it didn't update. I went an refreshed the system and I could now see all changes. Again, refreshing is not what the normal business owner will think to do. [li]Yes, the billing could show pricing so the client can see at a glance what they are. It would be even better if they can upgrade/downgrade/cancel themselves with the link to stripe etc.

title: Directly link to the Google Review form in GooglePlaces
content:
You can link directly to the review form for a business on Google Places \(now part of Google Business Profile\) by following these steps:

1. Find a Business on Google: Search for the business on Google Maps or Google Search.

2. Get the Place ID: - Use the Google Place ID Finder \([https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/javascript/examples/places-placeid-finder](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/javascript/examples/places-placeid-finder)\) to find the Place ID. - Enter the business name and address, and it will give you the Place ID.

3. Create the Review Link: Once you have the Place ID, you can create a direct link to the review form using the following format: [https://search.google.com/local/writereview?placeid=YOUR_PLACE_ID](https://search.google.com/local/writereview?placeid=YOUR_PLACE_ID)[ ](https://search.google.com/local/writereview?placeid=YOUR_PLACE_ID%E2%80%A8Replace)Replace YOUR_PLACE_ID with the actual Place ID you obtained.

4. Install your NFC product/Share the Link: You can now install your NFC product or share this link with customers, and when they click it, they'll be taken directly to the review form for the particular business.

This method makes it easy for you to get acces to the review link of prospects/customers so you don't have to wait for them to hand you over their review link \(Saves you a lotttttt of time\).

This works perfectly when you show a prospect how the NFC card works to get them more reviews and the review page of their business pops-up. Watch how their behavior shifts from I'm really busy into let me tell you all about that one bad review we got that still got him/her cooked and their other visibilty challenges......now you're in!

Note: The attached pdf on how to install your NFC product is also added in [community resources](https://www.skool.com/climbo/classroom/586d15b5?md=28710c81fe084fd0ad6387a58d83a952).

title: Building a reviews-only business with Climbo — would love advice & connection
content:
TL;DR:
UK-based, turning 50, pivoting from a physical business due to health. Long-time Climbo believer, starting a reviews-only service and looking to learn from others doing the same — especially around pricing and sustainability.

Hi everyone 👋

I’ve been sitting with whether to post this for a while, so here goes.

A bit about me for context. I’m in the UK, recently turned 50, and I currently run a children’s football coaching business. I’ve had ongoing health issues through much of my life, which has made it difficult to hold down a traditional long-term career. Because of that, I’ve worked for myself for many years and have run my football coaching business for nearly a decade.

While I love what I do, I’m becoming increasingly aware that physically running sessions long-term may not be sustainable for me, so I’m now looking to pivot into something I can manage better alongside my health.

I actually picked up Climbo around three years ago and have watched it develop over time. I’ve always believed in the product — I just haven’t quite found the right moment or structure in my life to fully lean into it… until now.

What’s driving this for me isn’t just “starting an agency”. It’s also about rebuilding connection, confidence, and purpose.

I don’t have a big social or professional network anymore. I love meeting people and helping others win, but over time life has narrowed things a bit. I see this as an opportunity to:

[ul][li]build real relationships with local businesses[li]help people get tangible wins \(visibility, trust, confidence\)[li]and, honestly, improve my own mental health through meaningful work and community

I’m intentionally starting with a reviews-only / reputation-focused service. I want to get really good at one thing, deliver it well, and help businesses see clear wins. Over time, I’m very open to networking, learning new skills, or partnering with others for things like websites or SEO where it makes sense — but my aim right now is to keep the offer simple and do it properly.

title: 🚀 Development Update — What we’re working on this week
content:
Hi Climbers,

Here’s a quick update on what the team is building right now. A lot of powerful things are coming that will help you sell more, retain clients better, and show value more clearly.

🔧 New Features in Development

AI Activity
A new section where your clients can see everything the AI agents are doing automatically \(replies, posts, actions, etc.\).
Perfect to show the real work happening in the background and reinforce the value of your service.

Free Trial Banner
Trial accounts will see a top banner with custom text + button \(for example:
“You are on free trial. To upgrade, add a payment method. Click here.”\)
This will help increase trial → paid conversions.

Custom AI Prompt
You’ll be able to add custom instructions to AI agents \(tone of voice, style, brand personality\) so replies truly match your client’s voice.

Video on Review Link
Upload a short welcome video on the first page of the review link \(business owner greeting + asking for a review\).
This can significantly increase review conversion.

Connect Google during Onboarding \(skippable\)
During client onboarding, after adding the first location, they can connect their Google Business Profile immediately.
This means they enter the platform with all sections already filled.
Still optional — they can skip and connect later.

Reply to all past Google reviews
One-click bulk reply to old Google reviews \(with safe rate limits so Google doesn’t flag automation\).
Amazing for SEO and Google rankings.

🎓 New Tutorials & Resources

Automations Course
A brand new course on how to properly set up automations for your clients.

Mobile App Application Course
Step-by-step training on how to complete the mobile app application, create screenshots, and write promotional texts.

New Unbranded Client Tutorials + Chatbot
We’re filling the unbranded knowledge base here:
👉 [https://knowledge-base.help](https://knowledge-base.help)

It will include:

new unbranded tutorial videos

a chatbot trained on the docs

title: Any tips for businesses with no physical location?
content:
I work with some service-based businesses \(cleaners, trades, consultants, etc.\) who don’t have a physical address and operate purely as service-area businesses. I understand this can reduce visibility on Google, especially for local search and Maps, so I wanted to learn from people with hands-on experience.

A few questions for the group:

1\) From your experience, how significant is the visibility drop for service-area businesses without a physical address compared to similar businesses with a verified location?

2\) For businesses without a real office, what are the legitimate ways \(if any\) to get a location verified without risking suspension?

3\) Are there best-practice alternatives you’ve seen work well \(e.g. hiding address, shared offices, home address, virtual offices, etc.\)?

Would love to hear real data points or war stories, not just theory 🙏

title: Offering longer contracts for Climbo 2.0 software.
content:
I'd like to briefly return to the topic I raised this week during the live recordings:

Offering longer contracts for Climbo 2.0 software.

I agree with your comment that this is how modern customers in this disposable economy interact with SaaS software, BUUUUUTTTTTTTT ... At Climbo 1.0, I even spent time responding to customer reviews so they could see that the software is working for them.

I also supported them with newsletters, they could share with their staff, to keep the focus on gathering more reviews. I \(as we all do\) need to be compensated for that time, and that wouldn't work if my clients quit after, say, six months.

Is this something other Climbers experience?

Is this a reason for you [@Giacomo Chinellato](obj://user/edfd679f109c4ef4b46645bd3d030dcf) to consider offering a subscription model \(e.g., incorporating the subscription process in Stripe—similar to how you're now incorporating the "Free Trial Banner"\)?

title: Question about automating review flows (SMS / email / CRM)
content:
Hi everyone,

I’m currently working on improving my review software offering and I’m looking for some guidance from people who already have experience with review flow automation.

At the moment, I’m not yet helping my clients with integrations for automatically sending review requests \(such as SMS or email\), but this is something I want to start offering. The goal is to make the review process as automated as possible, without creating extra manual work for clients.

I know it’s possible to upload contacts via CSV, but that requires clients to do this repeatedly themselves. I’m not sure if customers are really willing to do that, or if it’s a scalable solution long term.

That’s why I’d love to learn from you:How do you automate review requests in practice?Do you connect review flows directly to a company’s CRM system?Do you use Zapier or other automation tools for this?If so, do you run this through your own Zapier account or through the client’s account?

I’m also curious about the cost side. If you send hundreds or thousands of review requests per month, do automation tools like Zapier become expensive? And how do you keep everything clean, structured, and easy to manage?

Any insights on what works best and how you’ve set this up would be hugely appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your help! 🙏

title: Idea: Making the Competitor Analysis Chart More Visual & Actionable
content:
Hi everyone

During yesterday’s AMA, I asked whether we could make the competitor analysis chart more visually appealing and easier to understand at a glance.
[@Giacomo Chinellato](obj://user/edfd679f109c4ef4b46645bd3d030dcf) suggested posting a proposal here in the community, so here we go 🙂

I used AI to generate a new visual concept for the chart. The main improvements I’m proposing are:

1\) Divide the chart into 4 quadrants
Each quadrant represents a clear position \(e.g. Hidden Gem, Market Leader, etc.\) and visually suggests where a business wants to be — ideally the Market Leader zone.

2\) Add axis labels
X-axis: Number of reviews
Y-axis: Average review score
Note: my AI stubbornly refused to add the Y-axis label in the generated image but conceptually it should be there.\)

3\) Actionable insight below the chart
Instead of just showing data, the chart also explains what to do next, e.g.: “18 reviews with 5 stars will move you to Market Leader.”

Curious to hear your thoughts 👀

Let’s discuss 👇

title: Opt-in Page → Instant Review Flow
content:
Really like the Opt-in Page functionality and have a quick suggestion. Currently, the opt-in page works well by collecting customer details \(name, email, phone\) and then triggering review requests via SMS/email.
One small enhancement that could significantly improve conversion:
👉 After the opt-in form is submitted, give an option to redirect the customer immediately to the review link page, instead of only relying on SMS/email.
Why this helps:
[ul][li]Many opt-ins happen right after a positive experience \(e.g., restaurant visit, clinic checkout\).[li]Customers are already present, engaged, and willing to leave a review at that exact moment.[li]Redirecting instantly to the review page reduces drop-offs caused by waiting for SMS/email.[li]SMS/email can still be sent in parallel as a backup or reminder.

title: Signed up 1st Person - on 14-day trial
content: No turning back now! Today I met with a Travel Agent. I simply asked her about her business & how her clients find her. She goes to events as a vendor. title: 🧠 𝙴𝚍𝚞𝚌𝚊𝚝𝚒𝚗𝚐 𝚌𝚞𝚜𝚝𝚘𝚖𝚎𝚛𝚜 - 𝙰 𝙶𝙴𝙽𝚄𝙸𝙽𝙴 𝙼𝙾𝙽𝙴𝚈-𝙼𝙰𝙺𝙸𝙽𝙶 𝙷𝙰𝙲𝙺 💶
content:
Educating customers is good.

Educating 𝙩𝙝𝙚𝙞𝙧 𝙘𝙪𝙨𝙩𝙤𝙢𝙚𝙧𝙨 is where the money is.

Most people here already know they should educate customers on how to use the platform.

Cool.

But if you actually want your customers to win \(and stay customers\)… you’ve got to go one layer deeper:

Teach them how to talk to 𝙩𝙝𝙚𝙞𝙧 customers.

Because the platform doesn’t create results.

The behaviors around it do.

And this one behavior is a ridiculously reliable “more reviews / more revenue” lever.

_____________________________________________________

A genuine money-making hack \(works in any tip-based business and in countries where tipping is not mandatory, like germany\)

I’ve used this across wildly different industries and watched companies go from 2 reviews to 200+ even without the use of an ingenious platform like Climbo, which makes life so much easier.

This works anywhere tips are common:

Hair salons, restaurants, barbers, auto repair, cleaners, plumbers, drain services… you name it.

If customers tip, this works.

_____________________________________________________

𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝘀𝗰𝗿𝗶𝗽𝘁 \(𝘀𝗶𝗺𝗽𝗹𝗲, 𝗵𝘂𝗺𝗮𝗻, 𝗵𝗶𝗴𝗵-𝗰𝗼𝗻𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴\)

When a customer offers a tip, the employee says:

“Thank you — that’s really kind.

You know what would help us even more than a tip?

A quick positive review on Google \(or wherever you found us\).

It helps us attract more customers like you — and that lets us keep improving our service.

It’s a small effort, but it would genuinely mean a lot.”

_____________________________________________________

That’s it.
No pressure.
No weird begging.
Just a sincere trade:
“If you want to help us, here’s the highest-leverage way.”

_____________________________________________________

𝗪𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗵𝗮𝗽𝗽𝗲𝗻𝘀 𝗻𝗲𝘅𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗳𝘂𝗻 𝗽𝗮𝗿𝘁

People love to help when you make it easy and give them a reason.

A meaningful percentage will leave the review right then and there.

And if you add a QR + NFC card so they can scan/tap in 2 seconds?
You can push compliance into the 80%+ range.

𝗕𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗽𝗮𝗿𝘁?

title: Option to disable Google in the review flow (while keeping it in dashboard/reporting)
content:
Hi [@Tommaso Chinellato](obj://user/27c01345df6742a69b73afc23a363774) , quick question.

I totally understand that Google is the most important platform and that it should always be available as an integration inside Climbo.

But why isn’t there an option to disable Google in the review flow?

Right now, if a restaurant wants to focus 100% on a TripAdvisor flow, Google still stays visible as a choice and people will often click Google after pressing 4 or 5 stars. That makes it hard to run a truly “TripAdvisor-first” campaign, because you can’t fully control the traffic and review volume going to TripAdvisor.

Ideally, Google reviews can still be tracked and shown in the dashboard for reporting, but the review flow could be set to push only TripAdvisor \(or only Trustpilot\) when that’s the main focus for a specific period.

title: 🥳 Climbo Restart — AMA recap + video recording
content:
Hey Climbers,
as promised, here’s the video where I recap everything we covered during the Climbo Restart AMA.

Unfortunately, the live recording of the event was lost \(my mistake — first live in a long time and I forgot to hit record 😅\), so I recorded this video to make sure no one misses anything important.

In the video you’ll find:

• a recap of the new features released this week and how our weekly development cycle works

• an overview of the new community structure and how we’ll use Skool going forward

• our updated mission and vision for Climbo and the Review Marketing Flywheel

• the new pricing model and why it’s built for long-term sustainability

• the presentation of the new mobile app service \(iOS & Android\), including how it works and who it’s for

About the mobile app, I want to address something directly.
I’ve seen mixed reactions: some of you think the price is fair, others feel it’s high. Both reactions are totally understandable.

What’s important to clarify is this:
this is a done-for-you service. We don’t just give you an app template. We handle everything — development, store assets, submissions, revisions, and the back-and-forth with Apple and Google, which often requires multiple manual edits and approvals. That process alone can take weeks if you’ve never done it before.

That said, the mobile app is not mandatory.
Even without it, you already have a powerful platform with a great mobile web experience that works perfectly for most agencies and their clients.

The app is for those who want to take Climbo to the maximum level:
stronger brand authority, push notifications, higher client retention. It’s a leverage play — not something everyone needs on day one.

⚠️ Important: inside the video I also reveal a secret gift: a limited coupon code that saves 60% on the mobile app cost for the first batch only. This is available only for early adopters who want to be part of the initial release.

Watch the video, take what’s useful for you, and feel free to ask questions in the comments.

title: Quick reminder for everyone in the community 👋
content:
Over the last days, a few posts were published with account-specific questions \(login issues, client account problems, bugs tied to a single account\). We totally understand the urgency in these situations — but we want to keep the community clean, useful, and valuable for everyone.

Please take a moment to re-read all the community rules, especially Rule #3:
👉 [https://www.skool.com/climbo/community-rules-please-read-before-posting](https://www.skool.com/climbo/community-rules-please-read-before-posting)

How to use the community correctly:

Use the Support section for general questions whose answers can help other Climbers.

For account-specific issues \(your setup, a specific client, billing, bugs\), always open a private support ticket inside the platform. This is the only way our support team can properly track and resolve the issue.

The community is here to:

• share wins

• exchange strategies

• learn from each other

• discuss product updates and best practices

Keeping it free from account-level support requests helps new members feel confident, keeps the signal high, and allows everyone to learn faster.

Thanks for helping us keep this a positive, focused, high-value space 🙏

title: Just a heads up — this is a bit of a long post.
content:
Hey everyone — I’m new here and new to SaaS.

I’ve spent most of my working life in a local service business \(painting in NZ\) and I’ve always wanted to build something online. I recently launched my site and have been running Meta ads \(plus a bit of Google Ads\) for just over a month, but I haven’t seen any real conversions yet.

For context: I originally planned to use the platform to serve my local region, but I later learned the setup I needed doesn’t really work from my area. That took a few months to discover during prep. No hard feelings — I get that regional limitations can be messy and sometimes you only find out once you’re deep in it. It just meant I had to pivot and focus on an overseas market \(starting with the US\).

One thing that has been a big win \(and what gave me confidence in this space\) is that I used my own painting business as a case study. We got 19 x 5-star reviews in 14 days and we’re now sitting at 22 x 5-star reviews. That lift alone has helped bring in roughly 300 Google searches per month, and we’re currently ranking around #6 in our area for painting. It’s also been a cool real-world moment meeting people who need a painter — I tell them to look us up and they’re like “oh wow… so many 5-star reviews” 😂 That honestly was pure joy and I want to help other businesses get that same result.

I’m also working with someone in the US to finish setting up a cold outreach system to add to my marketing efforts.

Question: Has anyone here successfully acquired customers in a different country/overseas market? If so, what actually worked for you \(and what didn’t\)?

I’ve seen a few threads where posting in local Facebook groups has worked really well — but since I’m not local in the US, that approach feels harder to pull off authentically. I’ve invested a fair bit into the website + marketing so far, and I’m honestly just looking for a “first win” to build momentum again.

Also, I saw the live event timing is around 4am for me in NZ, but I heard the live will be posted after — so I’ll catch the replay.

title: 🎙️ Weekly AMA — Every Thursday at 4:00 PM (Rome Time)
content:
Every Thursday at 4:00 PM \(Rome time\) we host our Weekly AMA, a live session designed to keep you aligned, informed, and moving fast.

Each session follows a simple structure:

Part 1 — New Features Demo \(≈15 minutes\)
We walk you through the latest features released that week, explain why they matter, and show you exactly how to use them with your clients.

Part 2 — Live Q&A \(≈45 minutes\)
Open floor for your questions. No slides. No fluff. Just real answers, real demos, and real guidance.

How to add the event to your calendar:
[ol:1][li]Go to 👉 [https://www.skool.com/climbo/calendar](https://www.skool.com/climbo/calendar)[li]Select the Weekly AMA event[li]Click Add to calendar
You’ll receive:
[ul][li]a reminder 1 day before the event[li]a notification when the event starts

You can join live or watch the recording afterward if you can’t attend.

If you want to build consistent MRR with Climbo, this is your weekly anchor.

title: Creating a free trial without email
content:
In Climbo 1.0, we had the option to create a free trial for a customer without entering an email ID.
In Climbo 2.0, the email ID is mandatory. However, in many cases, when a lead comes in, we may have the business name, GBP/listing, and contact details, but not the email address at that stage.
It would be really helpful if we could again have the option to create a free trial without an email ID \(like in 1.0\) and add the email later once available.
This would make lead onboarding much smoother.

title: Hello and info on my Funnel
content:
Hi all, I am new here and new to running reputation management \(I have had Climbo for ages, but have been internally scared to push this bigger, and I don't know why\).

title: Feature Request Roadmap - Tiered Pricing
content:
Hi [@Giacomo Chinellato](obj://user/edfd679f109c4ef4b46645bd3d030dcf), I saw that in the Feature Request Roadmap the Agent feature Tiered Pricing is planned.

In Climbo 1.0 our reputation management service is build on tiers.
For example:
Max of 3 Locations € 77
Max of 10 Locations € 197
Max of 30 Locations € 497

We want to offer the same in Climbo 2.0

Is there already a workaround that I can still offer these kind of plans?
or
Can this Agent feature be prioritized so we can transfer these clients to the new plan without any hassle?

Thanks in advance!

title: Refresh Google Reviews
content: [@Giacomo Chinellato](obj://user/edfd679f109c4ef4b46645bd3d030dcf) Is there a way, like in Climbo 1, to refresh Google reviews in Climbo 2 in order to have the most recent reviews ?

title: SMS ⚡️ (change name sender)
content: Currently to Review - is it possible to change the sender \(as in Climbo 1?\) or connect your sender.

title: New to SAAS business model
content: Hello, I am starting my reputation curation business and I'm so excited for the opportunity. I live in Colorado. My agency is focused on Review Management. My first client is my short term rental business.

title: 🔴 “Climbo Restart” — January 15th at 4:00 PM (Rome Time)
content:
This is a key moment for Climbo and for everyone building their business on the platform. During this live event, we’ll walk you through everything that has been built, where we are going, and how this restart impacts you directly.

During the session, we will cover:

A full live demo of Climbo 2.0, including all the features developed over the past months.
Our new website and pricing model, designed around long-term, recurring revenue.
The new community structure, including rules, classroom organization, live sessions, and rewards.
Our updated vision and mission, and where Climbo is heading over the next years.
A new surprise feature, including a secret gift, designed to significantly raise the bar for review management and long-term agency retention.

Before the event — please read carefully

We kindly ask everyone not to flood the community with questions, complaints, or speculation before the live session. The event is designed to give clear, structured answers to all the major topics in one place, with full context.

If you can join live, there will be a dedicated Q&A at the end where you can ask your questions directly and get real-time answers.

If you can’t attend live, no problem at all. The full event will be recorded and uploaded in the Classroom so you can watch it later.

For now, please do the following:

Click on the “Live A.M.A.” event above this post and add it to your calendar.
Hold your bigger questions until the live session so we can address everything properly.
Join live if possible, or watch the replay afterward in the Classroom.
Feel free to introduce yourself in the Intros category — tell us who you are, what kind of agency you run, and what you’re aiming to build.

This is a real restart. A better product, a clearer direction, and a stronger long-term commitment.

We’ll see you live at Climbo Restart.

title: [...]Stories Sharing
content:
Will it be possible in the future to plan and post Instagram, Facebook \(and TikTok\) stories and posts that you have created yourself?
I really liked the idea and implementation from the past.

The roadmap states:
"\[...\] Stories Sharing
\(Clients Mode\)
Share reviews as Instagram Stories with ready-to-post, branded visuals."

Will it only be possible to post reviews, or will it also be possible to schedule your own videos on social media channels?

Best regards :\)
